message.content:
You are a DIRTY-ROOM integrator for the tamarin-rs relicensing campaign, working in /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs. Read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md and the relevant INTEGRATION_REPORT.md sections (waves 2-3 document each blocker this round aimed to close).
RULES: adapters and extraction only — NEVER transplant ported logic into clean (headerless) files; clean vendored sources get mechanical fixes only. A clean file acquiring a GPL header from scripts/gen_license_headers.py is a provenance violation: stop and report. If a gap still blocks, KEEP the ported path + report precisely. Comments describe current code only. No git commits. Append a dated section to INTEGRATION_REPORT.md; return a plain-text summary.
FINAL VALIDATION: cargo build --workspace; cargo test -p tamarin-parser -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-prover -p tamarin-server; wellformedness fixture suite; scripts/gen_license_headers.py then --check (0 stale). Report the header-count delta and author citations that disappeared. If you must stop partway, leave the tree BUILDING AND GREEN and report the precise remaining steps.

Unit A — full adoption of web_clean::dispatch as the server's request path (blocked in wave 3 on the sync-dispatch serialization conflict; round-7 redesigned dispatch to snapshot->compute->commit with per-request concurrency semantics probed from the reference, audit PASSED). D and B integrators ran before you; rebase on the CURRENT tree. This is the campaign's largest single win (deleting routes.rs/state.rs dispatch erases the last pseudonymous web authors) — proceed deliberately.
1. Re-sync web_clean from the round-7 workspace (mechanical, headerless).
2. Implement StateOps over the ported state::TheoryStore honoring the snapshot/commit contract (clone-on-read maps the ported mutex-cloning get; commit-allocates maps the counter semantics; NO lock held across ProverOps compute — the ported spawn_blocking offload pattern slots into the compute step).
3. Implement ProverOps over the ported pure producers (theory_html::{overview_page,path_html,proof_html}, render_theory_source, title_for, next/prev_theory_path, root::render_index, the proof-step/autoprove logic in handlers/theory.rs — extraction of ported code into pure functions is allowed and stays headered).
4. Route ALL routes through the clean dispatch with axum as transport glue; wire the origin-aware shell (capture an uploaded-overview parity fixture from the live reference first so the non-local branch is byte-gated — wave-3 report flagged this exact missing fixture).
5. Gates: every route test + captured-HS parity fixture green (routes_stubs del_path/verify included); concurrent-behavior sanity (a slow autoprove must not block unrelated GETs — add/adapt a test).
6. When the ported routing/dispatch surfaces (routes.rs route table, handler dispatch shells, state.rs version-allocation logic now expressed through the clean contract) are no longer referenced, DELETE them and rename web_clean -> web. Expect a header drop; report exactly which files and author citations disappeared. If the concurrency contract still cannot be honored, stop, keep ported, report the precise conflict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

243	fragment producer; the two share `type Theory`. `remove` (deletion) is on the trait
244	for backend ownership completeness but is **not invoked by any current route** — under
245	the observed retention invariant the web layer never drops a version; it is documented
246	as a contract-surface method, not a live path. The trait is a synchronous facade (its
247	`get` hands out a borrow); the consumer's async cache adapts behind it — the contract
248	this round pins is the *lifecycle*, which is what the observed behaviour constrains.
249	
250	### Deliverables
251	
252	- `src/page.rs` — `Origin` enum; `PageParams.origin`; `reload_item`/`append_item`.
253	- `src/shell_template.rs` — `RELOAD_ITEM` + `§RELOAD§` slot (Reload item was
254	  previously hardcoded); `APPEND_ITEM`/`§APPEND§` now gated on (kind, origin).
255	- `src/dispatch.rs` — `StateOps` trait + `InMemoryState`; `Server<P, S>` refactor
256	  (`new`/`with_state`); `Meta.origin`; `get_overview` threads origin.
257	- `tests/parity.rs` (+4 origin byte-parity tests, +`assert_shell_reproduces`),
258	  `tests/dispatch6.rs` (7 tests), four `r6_overview_*` fixtures; the round-3/4/5
259	  `FakeProver`s gained `Meta.origin`.
260	- BEHAVIOR.md §16; QUERIES.log [R60]–[R63].
261	
262	`cargo test` → **102 passing** (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14 dispatch5 +
263	7 dispatch6 + 23 parity), `cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean. Every ITEM-1 body
264	was live-captured and reproduced byte-for-byte; the fresh-server determinism check
265	confirms reproducibility.
266	
267	---
268	
269	## Round 7 — concurrency-safe dispatch (snapshot → compute → commit)
270	
271	Adoption blocker: the round-6 `Server::dispatch(&mut self)` required exclusive
272	ownership per request, so behind one lock a long `autoprove` (seconds-to-minutes)
273	would freeze the whole server. Black-box only: live probing ([R70]–[R76], ports
274	3100/3101, Tutorial/NSLPK3/NAXOS_eCK/RYY_PFS trace + the `sapic/slow` PKCS11 theory
275	as the long op) plus three committed probe scripts (`scratch/r7/*.sh`). No file under
276	`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read. All live servers stopped.
277	
278	### STEP 1 — the reference's concurrency contract (probed; BEHAVIOR §17)
279	
280	Using PKCS11 `cannot_obtain_key_ind` (~30s, terminating) as an in-flight long op:
281	
282	* **Fully non-blocking ([R71]).** A burst fired ~2.5s into the 30s autoprove — reads
283	  on other theories, reads on the SAME theory being proved, reads of other versions, a
284	  second proof op on a different theory, a second proof op on the SAME theory, an
285	  upload (`POST /`), and a reload (incl. the same base index) — every one returned in
286	  0.02–0.52s, all `200`, all long before the long op finished. Nothing blocked.
287	* **Commit-time allocation ([R72]).** The long op STARTED first (t=0.014s) but
288	  COMMITTED last (t=30.57s) and got the HIGHEST index (10); the two fast ops that
289	  committed at t≈2.6s got the lower indices 8/9. Indices follow **completion** order.
290	* **Invisible until commit ([R73]).** Index-page polling across the whole 30s never
291	  showed the long op's index until after it completed — the to-be-allocated version is
292	  unresolvable during the computation.
293	* **Atomic under races ([R74]).** 12 truly-simultaneous fast proof ops allocated
294	  indices 12..23 — distinct, contiguous, zero collisions, zero skips.
295	* **Snapshot isolation ([R75]).** A long autoprove on idx3 completed fine (→ idx24)
296	  even though idx3 was reloaded in place mid-computation; the reload was itself served
297	  concurrently. Retention reconfirmed (a capped-out index still resolves `200`).
298	* Incidental ([R76]): autoprove can fail with `{"alert":"Sorry, but the autoprover ()
299	  failed!"}` — documented gap, not addressed this round (concurrency-scoped change).
300	
301	### STEP 2 — the redesign
302	
303	`Server::dispatch` now takes **`&self`** (one server shared across concurrent
304	requests). Each request runs **get-snapshot → compute → commit**: resolve + take a
305	cheap owned snapshot via `StateOps::snapshot` (lock released), run the `ProverOps`
306	call — including the slow ones — on the snapshot with **no state lock held**, then
307	commit atomically (`insert_new` allocates the fresh monotonic index **at commit**;
308	`replace` for in-place reload/edits). The only serialized sections are the
309	microsecond-scale atomic `StateOps` calls. Design note: I kept a single `dispatch`
310	entry point (not a static cheap/slow route split) because the snapshot→compute→commit
311	pipeline already isolates the lock-free compute from the two tiny atomic state calls,
312	and even a "read" route may invoke a non-trivial fragment producer — classifying
313	routes as cheap/slow would be a lossy approximation of "hold no lock during any
314	`ProverOps` call".
315	
316	`StateOps` was reshaped to an interior-mutability, snapshot-handing trait (all `&self`;
317	`get`→`snapshot` returns an owned copy; `entries`→`indices`+`snapshot`). The reference
318	`InMemoryState<T: Clone>` is now a `Mutex<BTreeMap + counter>`; each method holds the
319	lock only for the map/counter op, never across compute. `Server`/`InMemoryState`
320	therefore compose to `Send + Sync`, so a consumer wraps the server in an `Arc` and
321	serves requests from any async task; a real backend implements the same `&self` façade
322	over its own async cache. The probed lifecycle (commit-time monotonic allocation,
323	atomicity, retention, in-place mutation, snapshot isolation) is the documented contract.
324	
325	### Deliverables
326	
327	- `src/dispatch.rs` — `dispatch(&self)`; `StateOps` reshaped (`snapshot`/`indices`,
328	  all `&self`); `InMemoryState` behind a `Mutex`; every handler restructured to take
329	  the request's single snapshot and commit via a separate atomic `StateOps` call.
330	- `tests/dispatch7.rs` (2 tests) — a `GatedProver` whose `autoprove` parks mid-compute
331	  on a controllable gate: `slow_op_in_flight_is_non_blocking_and_commits_last` asserts
332	  the full probed interleaving (non-blocking read while parked; a fast op commits first
333	  → index 2; the parked op's index invisible/unresolvable pre-commit; on release it
334	  commits last → index 3; final set contiguous), and `concurrent_allocations_never_
335	  collide_or_skip` races 16 threads and asserts a contiguous unique index block ([R74]).
336	- `tests/dispatch6.rs` — `WrapState` custom backend + `InMemoryState` contract tests
337	  updated to the `&self` snapshot API. All round-3/4/5/6 tests unchanged in intent
338	  (`let mut s` → `let s`; `dispatch5`'s `body` helper takes `&Server`).
339	- BEHAVIOR.md §17 (+ §16.2 superseding note); QUERIES.log [R70]–[R76].
340	
341	`cargo test` → **104 passing** (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14 dispatch5 +
342	7 dispatch6 + **2 dispatch7** + 23 parity); `cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean;
343	`dispatch7` verified non-flaky over 20 consecutive runs. All prior byte-parity tests
344	stay green (the state reshape is behind `Server::new`/`InMemoryState`, unchanged output).
345	
346	### Round 7 — verification pass (resumed session)
347	
348	The round-7 snapshot → compute → commit dispatch (above) was re-verified end-to-end in a
349	fresh session and independently re-corroborated against the live reference (my own probe,
350	`QUERIES.log` [R77]), not relying on the prior [R70]–[R76] logs:
351	
352	- **Implementation matches the contract.** `Server::dispatch(&self)`; `StateOps` is an
353	  interior-mutability `&self` trait (`snapshot`/`indices`; `insert_new`/`replace`/`remove`);
354	  `InMemoryState<T>` is a `Mutex<BTreeMap + counter>` holding the lock only for the
355	  map/counter op; every handler runs get-snapshot → lock-free `ProverOps` compute →
356	  atomic commit. No state lock is held across any `ProverOps` call.
357	- **Tests.** `cargo test` → **104 passing** (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14
358	  dispatch5 + 7 dispatch6 + 2 dispatch7 + 23 parity); `cargo clippy --tests --examples`
359	  clean (rc 0); `dispatch7` **non-flaky over 30 consecutive runs**. All byte-parity tests
360	  stay green.
361	- **Live re-corroboration ([R77]).** On a fresh reference server (Tutorial/NSLPK3/
362	  NAXOS_eCK/RYY): (a) non-blocking — a 5-way burst during an in-flight autoprove all
363	  returned 200 in 0.02–0.34s, incl. a read of the same theory being proved; (b) commit-
364	  order allocation — the autoprove that STARTED first COMMITTED last and took the HIGHER
365	  index; (c) invisible-until-commit — a ~1.5s NAXOS autoprove's index (8) was absent from
366	  every poll during its compute and appeared only at the first post-commit poll,
367	  contiguous with no skip. All live servers stopped; 3100–3199 clear.
368	
369	No file under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read this session; the only tamarin-rs
370	touch was EXECUTING the sanctioned reference binary for live probing.

1	//! The interactive-UI **state machine**: route dispatch decisions, theory-version
2	//! management, and response assembly across the **whole** request surface.
3	//!
4	//! This owns the web layer's *behaviour* — what each route returns and how the
5	//! version state evolves — over a [`ProverOps`] callback trait supplied at
6	//! integration by the ported prover. This module never inspects a theory; it
7	//! only decides which callback to invoke, how the version state changes, and how
8	//! to wrap the result into an HTTP response.
9	//!
10	//! Everything here was derived from black-box observation (`BEHAVIOR.md` §§13–17,
11	//! `QUERIES.log`); no prover source was read.
12	//!
13	//! ## Surface owned here
14	//! * **Top level.** `/` (index GET / theory-upload POST), `/robots.txt`,
15	//!   `/favicon.ico` (a `303` to the static icon), `/kill` (cancel a running
16	//!   search — needs a `path` query arg), and `/static/**` (filesystem assets:
17	//!   content type by extension, `File not found` for a miss).
18	//! * **Theory scope** `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…` for both `trace` and `equiv`
19	//!   (observational-equivalence / diff) kinds: the read views, proof operations,
20	//!   structural edits, navigation, graph routes, plus `reload`, `download`,
21	//!   `get_and_append`, and the diff analogues `diffProof` / `diffMethod` /
22	//!   `diffrules` / `autoproveDiff` / `autoproveAll`.
23	//!
24	//! ## Version model (one global index space)
25	//! Every theory-version — an originally loaded theory, an uploaded theory, or a
26	//! version produced by a proof operation — occupies a distinct index in one
27	//! monotonically growing namespace. **Proof operations** (`method`, `diffMethod`,
28	//! `autoprove*`) allocate a **fresh** index (`= max ever + 1`) and leave earlier
29	//! versions resolvable. **Upload** likewise allocates a fresh index off the same
30	//! counter. **Structural edits** (`edit`/`add`/`delete`) and **`reload`** mutate
31	//! the theory **in place** at its index (no new index; the counter is untouched).
32	//! Navigation and read views never change the version set.
33	//!
34	//! ## Concurrency: snapshot → compute → commit (`BEHAVIOR.md` §17)
35	//! [`dispatch`](Server::dispatch) takes **`&self`**, so one [`Server`] is shared
36	//! across concurrent requests. The reference web UI serves a long-running proof
37	//! operation *without* freezing unrelated (or related) requests ([R71]); a slow
38	//! [`ProverOps`] call therefore must NOT hold any exclusive lock. Each request runs
39	//! as three phases:
40	//!
41	//! 1. **get-snapshot** — resolve the requested index and take a cheap owned
42	//!    snapshot of that version through [`StateOps::snapshot`], releasing the
43	//!    backend lock immediately.
44	//! 2. **compute** — run the [`ProverOps`] call (including the possibly-slow
45	//!    `autoprove*` / `apply_method` / `del_*` / `reload` / `load_theory` / edits)
46	//!    on the snapshot **with no state lock held**. Concurrent requests take their
47	//!    own snapshots and run in parallel.
48	//! 3. **commit** — apply the result with a separate, atomic [`StateOps`] call:
49	//!    [`insert_new`](StateOps::insert_new) for a proof op / upload / `del/path`
50	//!    (the fresh monotonic index is allocated **now**, at commit — matching the
51	//!    probed completion-order allocation), or [`replace`](StateOps::replace) for an
52	//!    in-place `reload`/structural edit. Reads commit nothing.
53	//!
54	//! The only serialized sections are the microsecond-scale atomic `StateOps` calls;
55	//! all compute is lock-free. Version state lives behind the [`StateOps`] backend's
56	//! interior mutability ([`InMemoryState`] is the reference in-memory implementation),
57	//! so a consumer can supply an asynchronous, internally-caching backend that remains
58	//! the single owner of theory state. The observed lifecycle (`BEHAVIOR.md` §17) —
59	//! commit-time monotonic allocation, atomicity under races, retention, in-place
60	//! mutation, snapshot isolation — is the documented **contract** that backend must
61	//! satisfy.
62	
63	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
64	use std::sync::Mutex;
65	
66	use crate::envelope::METHOD_FAILED_ALERT;
67	use crate::page::{Flash, Origin, PageParams, RootRow, ShellKind};
68	use crate::route::{
69	    Autoprove, AutoproveAll, AutoproveDiff, EditVerb, Handler, Index, Main, Nav, NavDir,
70	    OverviewView, Route, ThyPath, Toplevel,
71	};
72	use crate::{assets, envelope, errors, forms, intdot, page};
73	
74	/// `application/json; charset=utf-8`.
75	pub const CT_JSON: &str = "application/json; charset=utf-8";
76	/// `text/html; charset=utf-8`.
77	pub const CT_HTML: &str = "text/html; charset=utf-8";
78	/// `text/plain; charset=utf-8` (text bodies, `next`/`prev`, DOT, `robots`, `kill`).
79	pub const CT_TEXT: &str = "text/plain; charset=utf-8";
80	/// `application/octet-stream` (the `download` route).
81	pub const CT_OCTET: &str = "application/octet-stream";
82	
83	/// `Cache-Control` value on `303` redirects (and the favicon redirect).
84	pub const CACHE_CONTROL_NOCACHE: &str = "no-cache, must-revalidate";
85	/// `Expires` value paired with [`CACHE_CONTROL_NOCACHE`] (a fixed past instant).
86	pub const EXPIRES_PAST: &str = "Thu, 01 Jan 1970 05:05:05 GMT";
87	
88	/// HTTP request method (only the two the UI uses are modelled).
89	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone, Copy)]
90	pub enum HttpMethod {
91	    Get,
92	    Post,
93	}
94	
95	impl HttpMethod {
96	    fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
97	        match self {
98	            HttpMethod::Get => "GET",
99	            HttpMethod::Post => "POST",
100	        }
101	    }
102	}
103	
104	/// An incoming request. `path` is the URL path with any `?query` split off into
105	/// `query` (decoded key/value pairs).
106	pub struct Request<'a> {
107	    pub method: HttpMethod,
108	    pub path: &'a str,
109	    /// Decoded query-string pairs (e.g. `("path", "solve(...)")` for `/kill`).
110	    pub query: &'a [(String, String)],
111	    /// Decoded POST form fields (e.g. `("lemma-text", "lemma foo: …")`, or
112	    /// `("uploadedTheory", <source>)` for an upload).
113	    pub form: &'a [(String, String)],
114	}
115	
116	impl<'a> Request<'a> {
117	    pub fn get(path: &'a str) -> Request<'a> {
118	        Request { method: HttpMethod::Get, path, query: &[], form: &[] }
119	    }
120	    pub fn get_query(path: &'a str, query: &'a [(String, String)]) -> Request<'a> {
121	        Request { method: HttpMethod::Get, path, query, form: &[] }
122	    }
123	    pub fn post(path: &'a str, form: &'a [(String, String)]) -> Request<'a> {
124	        Request { method: HttpMethod::Post, path, query: &[], form }
125	    }
126	    fn field(&self, key: &str) -> &str {
127	        Self::lookup(self.form, key)
128	    }
129	    fn query_param(&self, key: &str) -> Option<&str> {
130	        self.query.iter().find(|(k, _)| k == key).map(|(_, v)| v.as_str())
131	    }
132	    fn lookup<'b>(pairs: &'b [(String, String)], key: &str) -> &'b str {
133	        pairs.iter().find(|(k, _)| k == key).map(|(_, v)| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("")
134	    }
135	}
136	
137	/// An outgoing response. `location` is set for `3xx` redirects; `no_cache`
138	/// requests the `Cache-Control`/`Expires` pair Yesod attaches to redirects.
139	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
140	pub struct Response {
141	    pub status: u16,
142	    pub content_type: &'static str,
143	    pub body: String,
144	    pub location: Option<String>,
145	    pub no_cache: bool,
146	}
147	
148	impl Response {
149	    fn base(status: u16, content_type: &'static str, body: String) -> Response {
150	        Response { status, content_type, body, location: None, no_cache: false }
151	    }
152	    fn json(body: String) -> Response {
153	        Response::base(200, CT_JSON, body)
154	    }
155	    fn html(body: String) -> Response {
156	        Response::base(200, CT_HTML, body)
157	    }
158	    fn text(body: String) -> Response {
159	        Response::base(200, CT_TEXT, body)
160	    }
161	    fn octet(body: String) -> Response {
162	        Response::base(200, CT_OCTET, body)
163	    }
164	    fn see_other(location: String) -> Response {
165	        Response { status: 303, content_type: CT_TEXT, body: String::new(), location: Some(location), no_cache: true }
166	    }
167	    fn not_found(echoed_path: &str) -> Response {
168	        Response::base(404, CT_HTML, errors::render_not_found(echoed_path))
169	    }
170	    fn invalid_args(messages: &[&str]) -> Response {
171	        Response::base(400, CT_HTML, errors::render_invalid_args(messages))
172	    }
173	    fn bad_method(method: HttpMethod) -> Response {
174	        Response::base(405, CT_HTML, errors::render_bad_method(method.as_str()))
175	    }
176	    /// The static-handler `404` for a missing file (plain text, distinct from the
177	    /// full-HTML `404` of the dynamic router).
178	    fn static_missing() -> Response {
179	        Response::base(404, CT_TEXT, assets::STATIC_NOT_FOUND.to_string())
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	/// Theory metadata the page shell needs.
184	pub struct Meta {
185	    pub name: String,
186	    pub version: String,
187	    pub filename: String,
188	    /// Where this version was loaded from (Local file vs `POST /` upload); gates
189	    /// the north-bar "Reload file"/"Append modified lemmas" items. Proof-derived
190	    /// versions inherit the base theory's origin.
191	    pub origin: Origin,
192	}
193	
194	/// Per-version index-page row data (the non-deterministic parts of a table row).
195	pub struct RootMeta {
196	    /// Load time, e.g. `"17:44:37"` (non-deterministic).
197	    pub time: String,
198	    /// Source origin, e.g. a temp path or the uploaded filename (non-deterministic).
199	    pub origin: String,
200	    /// Whether the theory has been modified from its loaded state.
201	    pub modified: bool,
202	}
203	
204	/// A rendered content pane (`html` + pane `title`).
205	pub struct Content {
206	    pub html: String,
207	    pub title: String,
208	}
209	
210	/// A request for a `main/*` content pane, resolved to the prover.
211	pub enum MainReq<'a> {
212	    Help,
213	    Message,
214	    Rules,
215	    Tactic,
216	    Cases { refined: bool, level: usize, n: usize },
217	    Lemma(&'a str),
218	    Proof { lemma: &'a str, path: &'a [String] },
219	    /// Diff-mode proof view.
220	    DiffProof { lemma: &'a str, path: &'a [String] },
221	    /// Diff construction/deconstruction rules view.
222	    DiffRules,
223	}
224	
225	/// The prover-supplied callbacks. The web layer treats every returned string as an
226	/// opaque fragment; the *decisions* around these calls (version allocation, route
227	/// dispatch, envelope shape, content types, redirects) live in [`Server`].
228	///
229	/// Callbacks receive a `&Self::Theory` **snapshot** (owned by the caller for the
230	/// duration of the call), never a live handle into the state backend; a possibly
231	/// slow call (`autoprove*`, `apply_method`/`apply_diff_method`, `del_*`, `reload`,
232	/// `load_theory`, edits) therefore runs with no state lock held (`BEHAVIOR.md` §17).
233	pub trait ProverOps {
234	    /// Opaque per-version theory handle held by the [`Server`].
235	    type Theory;
236	
237	    // ---- pretty-printers / fragment producers ----
238	    /// Shell metadata (theory name, Tamarin version string, source filename, and
239	    /// the load [`Origin`]). The prover is the authority on a version's origin: a
240	    /// proof-derived version reports the same origin as the base it came from.
241	    fn meta(&self, thy: &Self::Theory) -> Meta;
242	    /// Index-page row data (load time, origin, modified flag) for `thy`.
243	    fn root_meta(&self, thy: &Self::Theory) -> RootMeta;
244	    /// The `source`/`message`/`download` body: the theory source, verbatim.
245	    fn source_text(&self, thy: &Self::Theory) -> String;
246	    /// Inner HTML of the west proof-script pane at `index`.
247	    fn west_pane(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, index: u64) -> String;
248	    /// Center-pane content (html + title) for a `main/*` view at `index`.
249	    fn main_content(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, index: u64, req: &MainReq) -> Content;
250	    /// Raw (unescaped) current source of a lemma, for the edit form.
251	    fn lemma_source(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, name: &str) -> Option<String>;
252	    /// Graphviz DOT for an `interactive-graph-def` node.
253	    fn graph_dot(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, tail: &[String]) -> String;
254	    /// The `next`/`prev` target URL (bare path), from the prover's traversal.
255	    fn nav_target(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, index: u64, dir: NavDir, mode: &str, lemma: &str) -> String;
256	    /// The `get_and_append` alert message (names the file appended to).
257	    fn append_message(&self, thy: &Self::Theory) -> String;
258	    /// Raw bytes of a `/static/<path>` asset, or `None` if the file is absent.
259	    fn static_file(&self, path: &[String]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>;
260	
261	    // ---- mutations / loads ----
262	    /// Parse an uploaded theory source into a theory, or `None` on failure.
263	    fn load_theory(&self, source: &str) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
264	    /// Re-read the theory from its source in place (the `reload` route).
265	    fn reload(&self, thy: &Self::Theory) -> Self::Theory;
266	    /// Apply proof method `n` at `(lemma, path)`. `Some((theory, focus))` on
267	    /// success (`focus` = the prover's next open goal, incl. root `_`); `None`
268	    /// if the method application failed (the web layer answers a JSON alert).
269	    fn apply_method(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, lemma: &str, n: usize, path: &[String]) -> Option<(Self::Theory, Vec<String>)>;
270	    /// Autoprove per `spec`. Returns the new theory and resulting focus path.
271	    fn autoprove(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, spec: &Autoprove) -> (Self::Theory, Vec<String>);
272	    /// Edit lemma `name` in place. `Some` = the modified theory; `None` = parse/wf
273	    /// failure or unknown lemma (the web layer then re-renders the edit form).
274	    fn edit_lemma(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, name: &str, text: &str) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
275	    /// Add a lemma at position `pos`. `Some`/`None` as for [`Self::edit_lemma`].
276	    fn add_lemma(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, pos: &str, text: &str) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
277	    /// Delete lemma `name` in place. `Some` = the modified theory (redirect to the
278	    /// help view); `None` = lemma not found (redirect to the delete view).
279	    fn delete_lemma(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, name: &str) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
280	
281	    // ---- del/path + verify (theory-path operations) ----
282	    /// Whether `lemma` is a lemma present in `thy`. Drives the `verify/proof/…`
283	    /// redirect-vs-content choice: a `verify/proof/{lemma}[/path]` redirects to the
284	    /// proof view iff the lemma is present, otherwise the help pane is returned.
285	    fn lemma_present(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, lemma: &str) -> bool;
286	    /// Delete the proof at the lemma node `name` (`del/path/lemma/{name}`),
287	    /// producing a fresh theory version. `None` if the lemma cannot be removed
288	    /// (absent) — the web layer then answers a "removing the selected lemma failed"

307	/// The theory-version **state backend**: the single owner of the version set and
308	/// the monotonic version counter. [`Server`] holds no version map of its own and
309	/// drives all version state exclusively through this trait.
310	///
311	/// The trait is an **interior-mutability** façade (every method takes `&self`) so a
312	/// single [`Server`] can be **shared** across concurrent requests
313	/// ([`dispatch`](Server::dispatch) is `&self`). Reads take an owned **snapshot**
314	/// ([`snapshot`](StateOps::snapshot)) so the caller can run a possibly-slow
315	/// computation on it with no lock held; mutations
316	/// ([`insert_new`](StateOps::insert_new)/[`replace`](StateOps::replace)) are
317	/// separate atomic commits. A consumer can supply an asynchronous, internally-caching
318	/// backend that remains the sole owner of theory state ([`InMemoryState`] is the
319	/// reference in-memory implementation).
320	///
321	/// The lifecycle semantics below are the **contract** a backend must satisfy for the
322	/// observed web-UI behaviour to hold (probed live; `BEHAVIOR.md` §§13.1/14.3/17).
323	/// One global index namespace covers every loaded, uploaded, or proof-derived
324	/// version:
325	///
326	/// * **Commit-time monotonic allocation.** [`insert_new`](StateOps::insert_new)
327	///   stores the theory at `= (max index ever allocated) + 1`, never reusing an index
328	///   and independent of any base index. Allocation happens **at the call** (i.e. when
329	///   a proof op *completes*), not when the request begins, and is **atomic**: two
330	///   concurrent `insert_new` calls receive distinct, consecutive indices — never a
331	///   collision, never a skip (`BEHAVIOR.md` §17.2/§17.3). The first allocation on a
332	///   fresh backend is index `1` (the originally loaded theory).
333	/// * **Retention.** Every allocated index stays resolvable by
334	///   [`snapshot`](StateOps::snapshot) for the backend's lifetime; the web layer never
335	///   asks for a version to be dropped (the index-page window is a display cap only).
336	/// * **Snapshot isolation.** [`snapshot`](StateOps::snapshot) returns a cheap owned
337	///   copy that is unaffected by later mutations of the same index; a long compute run
338	///   on a snapshot is not corrupted by a concurrent [`replace`](StateOps::replace) of
339	///   its base (`BEHAVIOR.md` §17.4).
340	/// * **In-place mutation.** [`replace`](StateOps::replace) overwrites the theory at an
341	///   existing index (structural edits + `reload`) without allocating, and leaves the
342	///   counter and every other version untouched. The web layer only calls it on an
343	///   index it has already resolved.
344	/// * **Enumeration.** [`indices`](StateOps::indices) yields all allocated indices in
345	///   ascending order (drives the index page and [`Server::versions`]).
346	/// * **Deletion.** [`remove`](StateOps::remove) drops a version. Under the observed
347	///   retention contract the web layer never invokes it; it is part of the backend's
348	///   ownership surface (e.g. cache eviction) — see the honesty note in `REPORT.md`.
349	pub trait StateOps {
350	    /// The per-version theory handle, shared with [`ProverOps::Theory`].
351	    type Theory;
352	    /// Take a cheap **owned snapshot** of the theory at `index` (`None` if no such
353	    /// version exists), releasing any internal lock before returning so the caller
354	    /// can compute on it lock-free.
355	    fn snapshot(&self, index: u64) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
356	    /// Atomically allocate a fresh monotonic index, store `theory` there, and return
357	    /// the index (never reused; `1` on a fresh backend). Prior versions are retained.
358	    /// Concurrent calls never collide or skip.
359	    fn insert_new(&self, theory: Self::Theory) -> u64;
360	    /// Overwrite the theory at an existing `index` in place (no allocation; the
361	    /// counter and other versions are untouched).
362	    fn replace(&self, index: u64, theory: Self::Theory);
363	    /// Remove the version at `index` (returning it if present). Not exercised by the
364	    /// current web-layer surface — see the trait-level note.
365	    fn remove(&self, index: u64) -> Option<Self::Theory>;
366	    /// All allocated indices in ascending order.
367	    fn indices(&self) -> Vec<u64>;
368	}
369	
370	/// In-memory reference implementation of [`StateOps`]: a `BTreeMap` keyed by index
371	/// plus a monotonic counter, behind a [`Mutex`] for interior mutability. Satisfies
372	/// the full lifecycle contract and is the default backend used by [`Server::new`].
373	/// `snapshot`/`insert_new`/`replace`/`remove` each hold the lock only for the
374	/// map/counter operation itself, never across a [`ProverOps`] computation.
375	pub struct InMemoryState<T> {
376	    inner: Mutex<StateInner<T>>,
377	}
378	
379	struct StateInner<T> {
380	    versions: BTreeMap<u64, T>,
381	    next_index: u64,
382	}
383	
384	impl<T> InMemoryState<T> {
385	    /// An empty backend whose first [`StateOps::insert_new`] allocates index `1`.
386	    pub fn new() -> InMemoryState<T> {
387	        InMemoryState { inner: Mutex::new(StateInner { versions: BTreeMap::new(), next_index: 1 }) }
388	    }
389	}
390	
391	impl<T: Clone> InMemoryState<T> {
392	    /// A backend pre-seeded with `base` at index `1` (the originally loaded theory).
393	    pub fn seeded(base: T) -> InMemoryState<T> {
394	        let s = InMemoryState::new();
395	        let i = s.insert_new(base);
396	        debug_assert_eq!(i, 1, "the first allocated version index is 1");
397	        s
398	    }
399	}
400	
401	impl<T> Default for InMemoryState<T> {
402	    fn default() -> Self {
403	        InMemoryState::new()
404	    }
405	}
406	
407	impl<T: Clone> StateOps for InMemoryState<T> {
408	    type Theory = T;
409	
410	    fn snapshot(&self, index: u64) -> Option<T> {
411	        self.inner.lock().unwrap().versions.get(&index).cloned()
412	    }
413	    fn insert_new(&self, theory: T) -> u64 {
414	        let mut g = self.inner.lock().unwrap();
415	        let index = g.next_index;
416	        g.next_index += 1;
417	        g.versions.insert(index, theory);
418	        index
419	    }
420	    fn replace(&self, index: u64, theory: T) {
421	        self.inner.lock().unwrap().versions.insert(index, theory);
422	    }
423	    fn remove(&self, index: u64) -> Option<T> {
424	        self.inner.lock().unwrap().versions.remove(&index)
425	    }
426	    fn indices(&self) -> Vec<u64> {
427	        self.inner.lock().unwrap().versions.keys().copied().collect()
428	    }
429	}

431	/// The interactive server. It owns all route dispatch, transport, and response
432	/// assembly, but **not** the version state: that lives behind a [`StateOps`]
433	/// backend `S` (defaulting to the in-memory [`InMemoryState`]).
434	///
435	/// [`dispatch`](Server::dispatch) takes `&self`, so one `Server` is shared across
436	/// concurrent requests; every version read/allocation/mutation flows through `S`'s
437	/// interior mutability as a snapshot → compute → commit pipeline (see the module
438	/// docs and `BEHAVIOR.md` §17).
439	pub struct Server<P: ProverOps, S: StateOps<Theory = P::Theory> = InMemoryState<<P as ProverOps>::Theory>> {
440	    ops: P,
441	    state: S,
442	}
443	
444	impl<P: ProverOps> Server<P, InMemoryState<P::Theory>>
445	where
446	    P::Theory: Clone,
447	{
448	    /// Create a server holding `base` as version 1 in a fresh in-memory backend.
449	    pub fn new(ops: P, base: P::Theory) -> Server<P, InMemoryState<P::Theory>> {
450	        Server::with_state(ops, InMemoryState::seeded(base))
451	    }
452	}

454	impl<P: ProverOps, S: StateOps<Theory = P::Theory>> Server<P, S> {
455	    /// Create a server over a caller-supplied state backend. The backend must
456	    /// already hold the base theory at index 1 (see the [`StateOps`] contract);
457	    /// this is the seam a consumer uses to plug in its own theory-state owner.
458	    pub fn with_state(ops: P, state: S) -> Server<P, S> {
459	        Server { ops, state }
460	    }
461	
462	    /// Version indices currently allocated (ascending). All are resolvable.
463	    pub fn versions(&self) -> Vec<u64> {
464	        self.state.indices()
465	    }
466	
467	    /// Dispatch a request across the whole surface. Takes `&self`: safe to call
468	    /// concurrently on a shared server (each call snapshots, computes lock-free, and
469	    /// commits atomically — see the module docs).
470	    pub fn dispatch(&self, req: &Request) -> Response {
471	        match Toplevel::parse(req.path) {
472	            Toplevel::Root => self.root(req),
473	            Toplevel::Robots => self.get_only(req, || Response::text(assets::ROBOTS_TXT.to_string())),
474	            Toplevel::Favicon => self.get_only(req, || Response::see_other(assets::FAVICON_TARGET.to_string())),
475	            Toplevel::Kill => self.kill(req),
476	            Toplevel::Static(segs) => self.static_asset(req, &segs),
477	            Toplevel::Thy(route) => self.thy(req, &route),
478	            Toplevel::Other(_) => Response::not_found(req.path),
479	        }
480	    }

482	    /// Run `f` for a GET; a non-GET on a GET-only route answers `405`.
483	    fn get_only(&self, req: &Request, f: impl FnOnce() -> Response) -> Response {
484	        match req.method {
485	            HttpMethod::Get => f(),
486	            other => Response::bad_method(other),
487	        }
488	    }

490	    // ---- top-level routes ------------------------------------------------
491	
492	    fn root(&self, req: &Request) -> Response {
493	        match req.method {
494	            HttpMethod::Get => Response::html(self.render_index(Flash::None)),
495	            HttpMethod::Post => {
496	                let uploaded = req.field("uploadedTheory");
497	                // compute: parse the uploaded source (no state lock held).
498	                let loaded = if uploaded.is_empty() {
499	                    None
500	                } else {
501	                    self.ops.load_theory(uploaded)
502	                };
503	                match loaded {
504	                    Some(thy) => {
505	                        // commit: allocate a fresh index for the upload.
506	                        self.state.insert_new(thy);
507	                        Response::html(self.render_index(Flash::Loaded))
508	                    }
509	                    None => Response::html(self.render_index(Flash::PostFailed)),
510	                }
511	            }
512	        }
513	    }
514	
515	    /// Render the index page for the current version set. Each version is snapshotted
516	    /// (releasing the lock) and its row metadata produced lock-free; rows are emitted
517	    /// in ascending index order. The reference server's exact row *ordering* and the
518	    /// per-row time/origin are non-deterministic (prover/environment concerns).
519	    fn render_index(&self, flash: Flash) -> String {
520	        let mut version = String::new();
521	        // Owned strings must outlive the borrowed RootRow slice.
522	        let mut owned: Vec<(u64, String, String, bool, String)> = Vec::new();
523	        for idx in self.state.indices() {
524	            let thy = match self.state.snapshot(idx) {
525	                Some(t) => t,
526	                None => continue, // dropped between the index list and the snapshot
527	            };
528	            let m = self.ops.meta(&thy);
529	            let rm = self.ops.root_meta(&thy);
530	            if version.is_empty() {
531	                version = m.version.clone();
532	            }
533	            owned.push((idx, m.name, rm.time, rm.modified, rm.origin));
534	        }
535	        let rows: Vec<RootRow> = owned
536	            .iter()
537	            .map(|(idx, name, time, modified, origin)| RootRow {
538	                index: *idx,
539	                name,
540	                time,
541	                modified: *modified,
542	                origin,
543	            })
544	            .collect();
545	        page::render_root(flash, &version, &rows)
546	    }

550	            HttpMethod::Get => match req.query_param("path") {
551	                Some(_) => Response::text(assets::KILL_CANCELED.to_string()),
552	                None => Response::invalid_args(&[assets::KILL_NO_PATH_MSG]),
553	            },

558	    fn static_asset(&self, req: &Request, segs: &[String]) -> Response {
559	        match req.method {
560	            HttpMethod::Get => match self.ops.static_file(segs) {
561	                Some(bytes) => {
562	                    let joined = segs.join("/");
563	                    // Asset bytes are opaque environment data; a UTF-8 asset is
564	                    // reproduced exactly, a binary asset is streamed at integration.
565	                    let body = String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes).into_owned();
566	                    Response::base(200, assets::static_content_type(&joined), body)
567	                }
568	                None => Response::static_missing(),
569	            },
570	            other => Response::bad_method(other),
571	        }
572	    }

574	    // ---- theory-scoped routes -------------------------------------------
575	
576	    fn thy(&self, req: &Request, route: &Route) -> Response {
577	        // get-snapshot: resolve the index and take one owned snapshot of the version.
578	        // A `#` (current) index never resolves; an unknown index is a 404. This is the
579	        // only state read for the whole request; the compute below runs on the snapshot
580	        // with no lock held.
581	        let index = match route.index {
582	            Index::Num(v) => v,
583	            Index::Current => return Response::not_found(req.path),
584	        };
585	        let thy = match self.state.snapshot(index) {
586	            Some(t) => t,
587	            None => return Response::not_found(req.path),
588	        };
589	        let kind = shell_kind(&route.theory_kind);
590	        match (req.method, &route.handler) {
591	            // Proof operations (allocate a new version) — intercept before reads.
592	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Main(Main::Method { lemma, n, path })) => {
593	                self.proof_method(kind, &thy, lemma, *n, path, false)
594	            }
595	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Main(Main::DiffMethod { lemma, n, path })) => {
596	                self.proof_method(kind, &thy, lemma, *n, path, true)
597	            }
598	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Main(m)) => self.get_main(index, &thy, m),
599	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Overview(tail)) => {
600	                self.get_overview(kind, index, &thy, &OverviewView::parse(tail))
601	            }
602	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Autoprove(tail)) => match Autoprove::parse(tail) {
603	                Some(ap) => {
604	                    // compute (possibly slow) on the snapshot, then commit a new version.
605	                    let (new_thy, focus) = self.ops.autoprove(&thy, &ap);
606	                    let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
607	                    Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_proof_path(kind, new, &ap.lemma, &focus, false)))
608	                }
609	                None => Response::not_found(req.path),
610	            },
611	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::AutoproveDiff(tail)) => match AutoproveDiff::parse(tail) {
612	                Some(ap) => {
613	                    let (new_thy, focus) = self.ops.autoprove_diff(&thy, &ap);
614	                    let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
615	                    Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_proof_path(kind, new, &ap.lemma, &focus, true)))
616	                }
617	                None => Response::not_found(req.path),
618	            },
619	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::AutoproveAll(tail)) => match AutoproveAll::parse(tail) {
620	                Some(ap) => {
621	                    let new_thy = self.ops.autoprove_all(&thy, &ap);
622	                    let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
623	                    Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&format!("/thy/{}/{}/overview/help", kind.path(), new)))
624	                }
625	                None => Response::not_found(req.path),
626	            },
627	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Next(tail)) => self.get_nav(index, &thy, NavDir::Next, tail, req.path),
628	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Prev(tail)) => self.get_nav(index, &thy, NavDir::Prev, tail, req.path),
629	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Source) | (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Message) => {
630	                Response::text(self.ops.source_text(&thy))
631	            }
632	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Download(_)) => {
633	                Response::octet(self.ops.source_text(&thy))
634	            }
635	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Intdot(tail)) => self.get_intdot(kind, index, &thy, tail),
636	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::InteractiveGraphDef(tail)) => {
637	                Response::text(self.ops.graph_dot(&thy, tail))
638	            }
639	            // Wrong method on GET-only theory routes.
640	            (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::Source)
641	            | (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::Message)
642	            | (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::Download(_)) => Response::bad_method(HttpMethod::Post),
643	
644	            // In-place mutating POSTs.
645	            (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::Reload) => {
646	                // compute: re-parse (possibly slow) on the snapshot, then commit in place.
647	                let new_thy = self.ops.reload(&thy);
648	                self.state.replace(index, new_thy);
649	                Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&format!("/thy/{}/{}/overview/help", kind.path(), index)))
650	            }
651	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::Reload) => Response::bad_method(HttpMethod::Get),
652	            (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::GetAndAppend(_)) => {
653	                Response::json(envelope::render_alert(&self.ops.append_message(&thy)))
654	            }
655	            (HttpMethod::Get, Handler::GetAndAppend(_)) => Response::bad_method(HttpMethod::Get),
656	            (HttpMethod::Post, Handler::Edit(tail)) => self.post_edit(kind, index, &thy, tail, req),
657	
658	            // del/path (both kinds) and verify (trace only). The theory-path parse
659	            // decides route-match (unparseable -> 404 for any method); a parseable
660	            // path with a non-GET method is a 405.
661	            (_, Handler::DelPath(segs)) => self.del_path(req.method, kind, &thy, segs, req.path),
662	            (_, Handler::Verify(segs)) => {
663	                if kind == ShellKind::Trace {
664	                    self.verify(req.method, index, &thy, segs, req.path)
665	                } else {
666	                    // `verify` is not registered for equiv theories: a miss for any method.
667	                    Response::not_found(req.path)
668	                }
669	            }
670	            _ => Response::not_found(req.path),
671	        }
672	    }

758	    // ---- proof operations: new version + JSON {redirect}, or {alert} on failure ----
759	    fn proof_method(&self, kind: ShellKind, thy: &P::Theory, lemma: &str, n: usize, path: &[String], diff: bool) -> Response {
760	        // compute (possibly slow) on the snapshot with no state lock held.
761	        let applied = if diff {
762	            self.ops.apply_diff_method(thy, lemma, n, path)
763	        } else {
764	            self.ops.apply_method(thy, lemma, n, path)
765	        };
766	        match applied {
767	            Some((new_thy, focus)) => {
768	                // commit: allocate the fresh index at completion.
769	                let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
770	                Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_proof_path(kind, new, lemma, &focus, diff)))
771	            }
772	            None => Response::json(envelope::render_alert(METHOD_FAILED_ALERT)),
773	        }
774	    }
775	
776	    // ---- del/path : delete a theory path (proof op — new version) ----
777	    /// Dispatch a `del/path/…` request. The theory-path tail parses mode-aware
778	    /// (`kind`); an unparseable tail is a `404` (for any method), a parseable tail
779	    /// with a non-GET method is a `405`. A deletable path (a lemma or proof node)
780	    /// allocates a fresh version and redirects to `overview/<same-path>`; every
781	    /// other outcome is a JSON alert whose text is selected by the path type.
782	    fn del_path(&self, method: HttpMethod, kind: ShellKind, thy: &P::Theory, segs: &[String], path: &str) -> Response {
783	        let diff = kind == ShellKind::Equiv;
784	        let thy_path = match ThyPath::parse(segs, diff) {
785	            Some(tp) => tp,
786	            None => return Response::not_found(path),
787	        };
788	        if method != HttpMethod::Get {
789	            return Response::bad_method(method);
790	        }
791	        match thy_path {
792	            ThyPath::Lemma(name) => match self.ops.del_lemma_path(thy, &name) {
793	                Some(new_thy) => {
794	                    let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
795	                    Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_lemma_path(kind, new, &name)))
796	                }
797	                None => Response::json(envelope::render_alert(envelope::DEL_LEMMA_FAILED_ALERT)),
798	            },
799	            ThyPath::Proof { lemma, path } => self.del_proof(kind, thy, &lemma, &path, false),
800	            ThyPath::DiffProof { lemma, path } => self.del_proof(kind, thy, &lemma, &path, true),
801	            ThyPath::Other => Response::json(envelope::render_alert(envelope::DEL_PATH_CANT_ALERT)),
802	        }
803	    }
804	
805	    fn del_proof(&self, kind: ShellKind, thy: &P::Theory, lemma: &str, path: &[String], diff: bool) -> Response {
806	        match self.ops.del_proof_step(thy, lemma, path, diff) {
807	            Some(new_thy) => {
808	                let new = self.state.insert_new(new_thy);
809	                Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_proof_path(kind, new, lemma, path, diff)))
810	            }
811	            None => Response::json(envelope::render_alert(envelope::DEL_PROOF_STEP_FAILED_ALERT)),
812	        }
813	    }
814	
815	    // ---- verify : re-render a theory path (trace only; never mutates) ----
816	    /// Dispatch a `verify/…` request (trace theories only — the caller answers a
817	    /// `404` in equiv mode). The theory-path tail parses in the trace grammar; an
818	    /// unparseable tail is a `404`, a parseable tail with a non-GET method a `405`.
819	    /// A `proof/{lemma}[/path]` whose lemma is present redirects to
820	    /// `overview/proof/{lemma}[/path]` at the **same** version; every other path
821	    /// (including a proof path to an absent lemma) returns the theory help pane.
822	    fn verify(&self, method: HttpMethod, index: u64, thy: &P::Theory, segs: &[String], path: &str) -> Response {
823	        let thy_path = match ThyPath::parse(segs, false) {
824	            Some(tp) => tp,
825	            None => return Response::not_found(path),
826	        };
827	        if method != HttpMethod::Get {
828	            return Response::bad_method(method);
829	        }
830	        match thy_path {
831	            ThyPath::Proof { lemma, path } if self.ops.lemma_present(thy, &lemma) => {
832	                Response::json(envelope::render_redirect(&overview_proof_path(
833	                    ShellKind::Trace, index, &lemma, &path, false,
834	                )))
835	            }
836	            _ => {
837	                let content = self.ops.main_content(thy, index, &MainReq::Help);
838	                Response::json(envelope::render_content(&content.html, &content.title))
839	            }
840	        }
841	    }
842	
843	    // ---- structural POST: mutate in place; 303 (or 200 form on edit/add failure) ----
844	    fn post_edit(&self, kind: ShellKind, index: u64, thy: &P::Theory, tail: &[String], req: &Request) -> Response {
845	        let (verb, name) = match tail {
846	            [v, n] => match EditVerb::parse(v) {
847	                Some(verb) => (verb, n.clone()),
848	                None => return Response::not_found(req.path),
849	            },
850	            _ => return Response::not_found(req.path),
851	        };
852	        let k = kind.path();
853	        match verb {
854	            EditVerb::Delete => match self.ops.delete_lemma(thy, &name) {
855	                Some(new_thy) => {
856	                    self.state.replace(index, new_thy);
857	                    Response::see_other(format!("/thy/{k}/{index}/overview/help"))
858	                }
859	                // Lemma not found: theory unchanged, redirect to the delete view.
860	                None => Response::see_other(format!("/thy/{k}/{index}/overview/delete/{name}")),
861	            },
862	            EditVerb::Edit => {
863	                let text = req.field("lemma-text");
864	                match self.ops.edit_lemma(thy, &name, text) {
865	                    Some(new_thy) => {
866	                        self.state.replace(index, new_thy);
867	                        Response::see_other(format!("/thy/{k}/{index}/overview/edit/{name}"))
868	                    }
869	                    None => self.get_overview(kind, index, thy, &OverviewView::Edit(name)),
870	                }
871	            }
872	            EditVerb::Add => {
873	                let text = req.field("lemma-text");
874	                match self.ops.add_lemma(thy, &name, text) {
875	                    Some(new_thy) => {
876	                        self.state.replace(index, new_thy);
877	                        Response::see_other(format!("/thy/{k}/{index}/overview/add/{name}"))
878	                    }
879	                    None => self.get_overview(kind, index, thy, &OverviewView::Add(name)),
880	                }
881	            }
882	        }
883	    }
884	
885	    /// Apply a proof method that arrived as a parsed `Main::Method`. Exposed so a
886	    /// caller that pre-parses `main/method` can drive the version bump directly.
887	    /// Snapshots `index` internally (a `404` body if the version is absent).
888	    pub fn apply_main_method(&self, index: u64, lemma: &str, n: usize, path: &[String]) -> Response {
889	        match self.state.snapshot(index) {
890	            Some(thy) => self.proof_method(ShellKind::Trace, &thy, lemma, n, path, false),
891	            None => Response::not_found("method"),
892	        }
893	    }
894	}
895	
896	/// Map a URL theory-kind segment to a shell variant.
897	fn shell_kind(kind: &str) -> ShellKind {
898	    match kind {
899	        "equiv" => ShellKind::Equiv,
900	        _ => ShellKind::Trace,
901	    }
902	}
903	
904	/// Build a `del/path/lemma/{name}` redirect target: the `overview/lemma/{name}`
905	/// URL at version `index`.
906	fn overview_lemma_path(kind: ShellKind, index: u64, name: &str) -> String {
907	    format!("/thy/{}/{}/overview/lemma/{}", kind.path(), index, name)
908	}
909	
910	/// Build the redirect target for a proof operation: an `overview/proof/…` URL for
911	/// trace, or `overview/diffProof/…` for diff, at version `index`.
912	fn overview_proof_path(kind: ShellKind, index: u64, lemma: &str, focus: &[String], diff: bool) -> String {
913	    let handler = if diff { "diffProof" } else { "proof" };
914	    let mut s = format!("/thy/{}/{}/overview/{}/{}", kind.path(), index, handler, lemma);
915	    for seg in focus {
916	        s.push('/');
917	        s.push_str(seg);
918	    }
919	    s
920	}

8	
9	//! HTTP server for the Tamarin prover (Rust port) interactive UI.
10	//!
11	//! Goal: serve the existing `frontend/` (TypeScript + d3 + viz-js)
12	//! and the static assets under `data/` (jQuery, CSS, images) without
13	//! modifying any frontend code.  The route shape closely mirrors
14	//! Haskell's `Web.Dispatch` — same URL layout and the same JSON
15	//! response envelope (`{ html, title }` / `{ alert }` / `{ redirect }`)
16	//! — with one Rust-specific addition: `/thy/trace/:idx/proof-step/*path`
17	//! for the progressive UI, which has no counterpart in Haskell's route
18	//! table (`Web/Types.hs`).
19	//!
20	//! Wiring:
21	//!
22	//!   `tamarin-prover interactive theory.spthy`  →
23	//!     - load theory via `tamarin_parser::parse_theory`
24	//!     - elaborate via `tamarin_theory::elaborate::elaborate`
25	//!     - boot Maude (`tamarin_term::maude_proc::MaudeHandle`)
26	//!     - register the theory in a `TheoryStore`
27	//!     - serve the bundled `frontend/dist/` (+ `data/`) on a TCP port
28	//!
29	//! Routes (subset, matching Haskell):
30	//!
31	//!   GET  /                                                  RootR
32	//!   POST /                                                  RootR (file upload)
33	//!   GET  /thy/trace/<idx>/overview/*path                    InteractiveOverviewR
34	//!   GET  /thy/trace/<idx>/main/*path                        TheoryPathMR
35	//!   GET  /thy/trace/<idx>/source                            TheorySourceR
36	//!   GET  /thy/trace/<idx>/autoprove/<ext>/<bound>/<quit>/*p AutoProverR
37	//!   GET  /thy/trace/<idx>/unload                            UnloadTheoryR
38	//!   GET  /static/*                                          StaticR (serve data/ + frontend/dist/)
39	//!   GET  /favicon.ico                                       FaviconR
40	//!   GET  /robots.txt                                        RobotsR
41	//!
42	//! Implemented: most trace-theory routes are wired (see `routes.rs`),
43	//! including `overview`, `main`, `source`, `message`, `autoprove`,
44	//! `autoproveAll`, `verify`, `next`, `prev`, `download`, `reload`,
45	//! `get_and_append`, `proof-step`, `del/path`, `unload`, and graph
46	//! rendering (`intdot`/`graph`/`interactive-graph-def` render live SVG
47	//! via the DOT pipeline, with a DOT-text fallback).
48	//!
49	//! Stubs (return a JSON `{alert}` envelope, HTTP 200):
50	//!   - diff theories (`/thy/equiv/...`)
51	//!   - lemma editing (`edit`)
52	
53	// Sanctioned stdout path: the interactive server prints its "server ready at
54	// …" / "shutting down…" startup+lifecycle messages to stdout by design
55	// (mirroring HS's `Interactive.hs` ready message).  These are not the batch
56	// `--prove` byte-parity surface, so `println!` is the intended mechanism and
57	// the `disallowed_macros` freeze is allowed for this file.

64	// abbreviation); `web_clean` reimplements the interactive web-UI response
65	// bodies (HTML shell, forms, 404, JSON envelopes, DOT skeleton). Mechanical
66	// path fixes only (`crate::` -> `super::`).

83	pub struct ServerConfig {
84	    /// Address to bind, e.g. `127.0.0.1:3001`.
85	    pub bind_addr: SocketAddr,
86	    /// Path to the `data/` directory (CSS, JS, images, fonts).
87	    pub data_dir: PathBuf,
88	    /// Path to the bundled frontend output (`frontend/dist/`), if any.
89	    pub frontend_dist: Option<PathBuf>,
90	    /// Path to the Maude binary.
91	    pub maude_path: String,
92	    /// Proof-search step budget threaded to `prove_lemma` for API
93	    /// compatibility. Currently a no-op: the solver bounds search by
94	    /// ID-DFS depth + wall-clock deadline (HS-faithful), so this value
95	    /// is accepted but ignored.
96	    pub max_steps: usize,
97	    /// `--derivcheck-timeout` for the dynamic message-derivation checks
98	    /// run at theory load (HS interactive default 5s; 0 disables).  Set
99	    /// from the CLI flag by `interactive` setup.
100	    pub derivcheck_timeout: u32,
101	    /// CLI `--stop-on-trace` (None = flag absent).  Merged with each
102	    /// theory's in-file `configuration:` block at `ProofState::new` time
103	    /// per HS `closeTheory` precedence (TheoryLoader.hs:640-666): the CLI
104	    /// value wins; the block is consulted only when this is `None`.
105	    pub stop_on_trace: Option<tamarin_theory::constraint::solver::context::CutStrategy>,
106	}
107	
108	impl ServerConfig {
109	    pub fn new(bind_addr: SocketAddr, data_dir: PathBuf, maude_path: String) -> Self {
110	        Self {
111	            bind_addr,
112	            data_dir,
113	            frontend_dist: None,
114	            maude_path,
115	            max_steps: 500,
116	            derivcheck_timeout: 5,
117	            stop_on_trace: None,
118	        }
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	/// Start the server, blocking until shutdown.
123	///
124	/// Initial theory files (paths) are loaded eagerly.
125	pub async fn serve(
126	    cfg: ServerConfig,
127	    theory_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
128	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {
129	    // The web UI renders every HTTP response at HS's web width (100/67),
130	    // not the CLI console width (110/73) — HS `getTheorySourceR` uses
131	    // `render` (HughesPJ default `style`) and every HTML fragment goes
132	    // through `renderHtmlDoc`, both width 100.  Set process-wide before
133	    // any rendering.  (Console-only `renderDoc` at 110 has no HTTP
134	    // analogue here.)
135	    tamarin_theory::pretty_hpj::set_display_width(
136	        tamarin_theory::pretty_hpj::WEB_LINE_LENGTH,
137	        tamarin_theory::pretty_hpj::WEB_RIBBON,
138	    );
139	
140	    // Retain each proof node's constraint `System` after expansion.  The
141	    // `--prove` CLI drops them post-expansion to keep RSS low (the text
142	    // proof never reprints a per-node system), but the interactive UI
143	    // renders the annotated system + applicable proof methods at every
144	    // proof path — HS keeps a `Just System` on every `IncrementalProof`
145	    // node.  Must be set before the first `autoprove` runs a search.
146	    tamarin_theory::constraint::solver::search::set_keep_sys(true);
147	
148	    let store = TheoryStore::default();
149	
150	    // Eager-load every command-line theory.  Per-theory stdout reporting
151	    // mirrors HS `loadTheories` (Web/Dispatch.hs:157-198): a non-empty
152	    // wellformedness report is echoed via `ppInteractive`
153	    // (Dispatch.hs:200-209), and a load failure prints the dashed
154	    // `reportFailure` block (Dispatch.hs:191-198) and skips the theory.
155	    for p in &theory_paths {
156	        match theory_io::load_from_path(p, &cfg.maude_path, cfg.derivcheck_timeout) {
157	            Ok(entry) => {
158	                let name = entry.name.clone();
159	                if !entry.wf_report.is_empty() {
160	                    let dashes = "-".repeat(78);
161	                    let report = tamarin_theory::pretty_theory::render_wf_error_report(
162	                        &entry.wf_report,
163	                    );
164	                    println!(
165	                        "{dashes}\nTheory file '{}'\n{dashes}\n\nWARNING: ignoring the following wellformedness errors\n\n{}\n{dashes}\n",
166	                        p.display(),
167	                        report.trim_end_matches('\n'),
168	                    );
169	                }
170	                let idx = store.insert(entry);
171	                tracing::info!(idx, ?name, path = ?p, "loaded theory");
172	            }
173	            Err(e) => {
174	                tracing::error!(error = %e, path = ?p, "failed to load theory");
175	                let dashes = "-".repeat(78);
176	                println!(
177	                    "{dashes}\nUnable to load theory file `{}'\n{dashes}\n\n{}\n{dashes}\n",
178	                    p.display(),
179	                    e,
180	                );
181	            }
182	        }
183	    }
184	
185	    let state = Arc::new(AppState {
186	        cfg: cfg.clone(),
187	        store,
188	    });
189	
190	    let app = router(state.clone());
191	    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(cfg.bind_addr).await?;
192	    // HS ready message (Interactive.hs:104, printed after all theories
193	    // load, Dispatch.hs:160) — note the trailing space after "at" and the
194	    // indented URL line.
195	    println!(
196	        "Finished loading theories ... server ready at \n\n    http://{}\n",
197	        cfg.bind_addr,
198	    );
199	    axum::serve(listener, app)
200	        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal())
201	        .await?;
202	    Ok(())
203	}
204	
205	async fn shutdown_signal() {
206	    let ctrl_c = async { let _ = tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await; };
207	    #[cfg(unix)]
208	    let term = async {
209	        // Degrade gracefully if the SIGTERM handler can't be installed
210	        // (e.g. resource limits): only ctrl_c drives shutdown, instead
211	        // of panicking at startup.  Mirrors the non-unix branch.
212	        match tokio::signal::unix::signal(
213	            tokio::signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())
214	        {
215	            Ok(mut s) => { s.recv().await; }
216	            Err(e) => {
217	                tracing::warn!(error = %e, "could not install SIGTERM handler; \
218	                    only ctrl_c will trigger shutdown");
219	                std::future::pending::<()>().await;
220	            }
221	        }
222	    };
223	    #[cfg(not(unix))]
224	    let term = std::future::pending::<()>();
225	    tokio::select! {
226	        _ = ctrl_c => {}
227	        _ = term => {}
228	    }
229	    println!("\ntamarin-prover: shutting down...");
230	}

1	// Currently GPL 3.0 until granted permission by the following authors:
2	//   jdreier, arcz, meiersi, felixlinker, Kanakanajm, cascremers,
3	//   YannColomb, rsasse, beschmi, addap, Mathias-AURAND, BTom-GH,
4	//   PhilipLukertWork, xaDxelA, symphorien, racoucho1u,

6	//   contributors (see upstream git history)
