8	//   src/Web/Handler.hs, src/Web/Theory.hs
9	
10	//! In-memory store of loaded theories, mirroring Haskell `TheoryMap`.
11	//!
12	//! Indexed by integer (1-based, matching Haskell's behaviour) — the
13	//! frontend reads/writes these indices in URLs like
14	//! `/thy/trace/<idx>/main/...`.
15	//!
16	//! We keep both the parser AST and the elaborated typed theory.  The
17	//! parser AST is needed by `prove_lemma`; the elaborated theory is
18	//! used for accessor helpers (lemma list, restriction count, …).
19	//!
20	//! Concurrency: `parking_lot::Mutex` — interactive single-user UI, no
21	//! need for an async lock.  Only the autoprover (`autoprove` /
22	//! `autoprove_all`) offloads its work onto `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`;
23	//! interactive single-step applies and proof-state materialization run
24	//! inline on the async handler thread.
25	
26	use parking_lot::Mutex;
27	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
28	use std::path::PathBuf;
29	use std::sync::Arc;
30	
31	use chrono::{DateTime, Local};
32	
33	use tamarin_parser::ast as p;
34	use tamarin_theory::theory::Theory as TypedTheory;
35	
36	use crate::handlers::proof_tree::ProofState;
37	
38	/// One loaded theory with bookkeeping.
39	#[derive(Clone)]
40	pub struct TheoryEntry {
41	    /// Stable index used in URLs.  Set by `TheoryStore::insert`.
42	    pub idx: usize,
43	    /// Theory name from the `.spthy` source.
44	    pub name: String,
45	    /// Parser AST — kept verbatim so `prove_lemma` has the same shape
46	    /// it was elaborated from.
47	    pub parser_theory: Arc<p::Theory>,
48	    /// Elaborated, typed theory — used for accessor helpers.  Wrapped
49	    /// in `Arc` so we can clone the entry cheaply.
50	    pub typed_theory: Arc<TypedTheory>,
51	    /// Where the theory came from.
52	    pub origin: TheoryOrigin,
53	    /// Load time for the UI.
54	    pub loaded_at: DateTime<Local>,
55	    /// True for the originally loaded copy (vs. ones produced by edits).
56	    pub primary: bool,
57	    /// The theory's wellformedness report, computed at load time by the
58	    /// same pipeline `--prove` runs (`theory_io::load_from_source`, mirroring
59	    /// `run.rs`'s `checkWellformedness`).  This is the single source of truth
60	    /// for both wellformedness renderings: the `/* WARNING: ... */` comment in
61	    /// the `source`/`message` routes (`format_wf_block`) and the
62	    /// `<div class="wf-warning">` header banner in the `help`/`overview` routes
63	    /// (`errors_html`).  Empty ⇒ no warnings (theory is well-formed).
64	    pub wf_report: Vec<tamarin_parser::wf::WfError>,
65	    /// HTML for the wellformedness warning banner shown in the theory
66	    /// page header (HS `errorsHtml`, rendered raw via
67	    /// `preEscapedToMarkup info.errorsHtml` at `src/Web/Theory.hs`).
68	    /// Populated from [`wf_report`](Self::wf_report) at load time,
69	    /// mirroring HS `makeWfErrorsHtml` (`src/Web/Handler.hs`), which wraps
70	    /// `renderHtmlDoc (htmlDoc $ prettyWfErrorReport report)` of the
71	    /// *closed* theory's wellformedness report in a `<div class="wf-warning">`.
72	    /// Empty string when the report is empty (HS `makeWfErrorsHtml [] = ""`).
73	    pub errors_html: String,
74	    /// Live proof state — built lazily on first request that needs it
75	    /// (theory load → only kept-around-but-empty until `ensure_proof_state`
76	    /// is asked for).  `None` here means "not yet built"; on first
77	    /// access we boot Maude and precompute the per-lemma initial
78	    /// systems.  Building this eagerly at load time would cost ~1s
79	    /// per theory for Maude startup + source precompute, which is
80	    /// fine but pushes start-of-server latency.
81	    pub proof_state: Option<Arc<ProofState>>,
82	}
83	
84	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
85	pub enum TheoryOrigin {
86	    /// Loaded from a path on disk.
87	    Local(PathBuf),
88	    /// Uploaded via POST `/`.
89	    Upload(String),
90	    /// Generated interactively (e.g. by an edit). Currently never
91	    /// constructed — placeholder for the unported interactive-edit
92	    /// path (HS `Interactive`).
93	    Interactive,
94	}
95	
96	impl TheoryOrigin {
97	    pub fn label(&self) -> String {
98	        match self {
99	            TheoryOrigin::Local(p) => p.display().to_string(),
100	            TheoryOrigin::Upload(n) => n.clone(),
101	            TheoryOrigin::Interactive => "(interactively created)".into(),
102	        }
103	    }
104	}
105	
106	#[derive(Default, Clone)]
107	pub struct TheoryStore {
108	    inner: Arc<Mutex<TheoryStoreInner>>,
109	}
110	
111	#[derive(Default)]
112	struct TheoryStoreInner {
113	    by_idx: BTreeMap<usize, TheoryEntry>,
114	}
115	
116	/// Next free store index: Haskell's `M.findMax + 1` (empty → 1).  Single
117	/// spelling shared by `insert` and `clone_at_new_idx_with` so they cannot
118	/// drift; `next_back()` is O(log n) (unlike `keys().last()`, which walks).
119	fn next_free_idx(inner: &TheoryStoreInner) -> usize {
120	    inner.by_idx.keys().next_back().map_or(1, |k| k + 1)
121	}
122	
123	impl TheoryStore {
124	    /// Insert a new theory and return the freshly assigned index.
125	    pub fn insert(&self, mut entry: TheoryEntry) -> usize {
126	        let mut inner = self.inner.lock();
127	        // Match Haskell's `M.findMax + 1` (BTreeMap max key); empty → 1.
128	        let idx = next_free_idx(&inner);
129	        entry.idx = idx;
130	        inner.by_idx.insert(idx, entry);
131	        idx
132	    }
133	
134	    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<TheoryEntry> {
135	        self.inner.lock().by_idx.get(&idx).cloned()
136	    }
137	
138	    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<TheoryEntry> {
139	        self.inner.lock().by_idx.values().cloned().collect()
140	    }
141	
142	    pub fn remove(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<TheoryEntry> {
143	        self.inner.lock().by_idx.remove(&idx)
144	    }
145	
146	    /// Clone the entry at `src_idx` into a fresh `idx`, marking the
147	    /// clone as non-primary (Haskell `primary = False` for modified
148	    /// theories — see `putTheory` in `src/Web/Handler.hs`).  Updates
149	    /// the clone's `loaded_at`.  Returns the new idx.
150	    ///
151	    /// Used by `autoprove`, `autoproveAll`, `del/path` — mirrors
152	    /// Haskell's `modifyTheory` which always allocates a new idx.
153	    ///
154	    /// The `proof_state` is dropped on clone: each idx version should
155	    /// have its own proof tree so mutations on one don't leak to the
156	    /// other (Haskell's `IncrementalProof` is value-typed, not shared).
157	    /// The new idx rebuilds proof state on first `ensure_proof_state`
158	    /// — that's a ~1s cost (Maude boot + source precompute) but
159	    /// preserves the version-fork semantics.
160	    pub fn clone_at_new_idx(&self, src_idx: usize) -> Option<usize> {
161	        // Drop the shared proof state — clone gets its own (rebuilt
162	        // lazily).  See doc comment above.
163	        self.clone_at_new_idx_with(src_idx, |_| None)
164	    }
165	
166	    /// Shared body of [`clone_at_new_idx`] and
167	    /// [`clone_at_new_idx_forking_proof_state`]: lock, clone the source
168	    /// entry, allocate a fresh idx, mark it non-primary, and re-derive its
169	    /// `proof_state` via `fork_proof` (the only line that differs between
170	    /// the two public methods).
171	    fn clone_at_new_idx_with(
172	        &self,
173	        src_idx: usize,
174	        fork_proof: impl FnOnce(&Option<Arc<ProofState>>) -> Option<Arc<ProofState>>,
175	    ) -> Option<usize> {
176	        let mut inner = self.inner.lock();
177	        let mut clone = inner.by_idx.get(&src_idx).cloned()?;
178	        let new_idx = next_free_idx(&inner);
179	        clone.idx = new_idx;
180	        clone.primary = false;
181	        clone.loaded_at = Local::now();
182	        clone.proof_state = fork_proof(&clone.proof_state);
183	        inner.by_idx.insert(new_idx, clone);
184	        Some(new_idx)
185	    }
186	
187	    /// Like [`clone_at_new_idx`] but, when the source idx has a
188	    /// materialised `proof_state`, also fork it into the clone — share
189	    /// the `ProofContext` (Maude handle + precomputed sources) but
190	    /// deep-copy the per-lemma trees so subsequent mutations on the
191	    /// clone don't leak back into the source.  Used by the method-apply
192	    /// route so the post-step proof tree contains the SAME tree shape
193	    /// as the source idx (i.e. retains all children produced by prior
194	    /// applied steps), rather than rebuilding a bare initial-state
195	    /// tree.  This mirrors Haskell's `modifyTheory` semantics, where
196	    /// `putTheory` puts the *modified* `ClosedTheory` (with its full
197	    /// `IncrementalProof`) at the new idx — not a fresh one.
198	    pub fn clone_at_new_idx_forking_proof_state(&self, src_idx: usize) -> Option<usize> {
199	        // Fork the proof state if present — preserves the source tree's
200	        // shape under a new Arc.  If the source never materialised a
201	        // proof state, the clone starts from scratch (`None`).
202	        self.clone_at_new_idx_with(src_idx, |ps| ps.as_ref().map(|p| Arc::new(p.fork())))
203	    }
204	
205	    /// Replace the entry at `idx` in place, keeping the idx the same.
206	    /// Mirrors Haskell `replaceTheory` (`src/Web/Handler.hs` —
207	    /// used by `reload` and `editProof`).  Like `replaceTheory`'s
208	    /// `M.insert idx newThy theories`, this inserts unconditionally
209	    /// (creating the entry if `idx` is currently absent), and forces
210	    /// `primary = false` to match `replaceTheory`'s hard-coded `False`
211	    /// for the `primary` field (so a reloaded theory shows as
212	    /// "Modified", not "Original").  Always returns `Some(idx)`.
213	    pub fn replace_at(&self, idx: usize, mut entry: TheoryEntry) -> Option<usize> {
214	        let mut inner = self.inner.lock();
215	        entry.idx = idx;
216	        entry.primary = false;
217	        inner.by_idx.insert(idx, entry);
218	        Some(idx)
219	    }
220	
221	    /// Get-or-build the live [`ProofState`] for `idx`. Builds it
222	    /// lazily on first call and stores it in the entry so subsequent
223	    /// requests reuse the same proof tree.
224	    pub fn ensure_proof_state(
225	        &self,
226	        idx: usize,
227	        cfg: &crate::ServerConfig,
228	    ) -> Result<Arc<ProofState>, String> {
229	        // Fast path: already materialised.  Clone out the `Arc<Theory>`
230	        // we need, then release the store lock before the ~1s
231	        // `ProofState::new` (Maude boot + source precompute) so unrelated
232	        // handlers — and other tokio workers — aren't blocked for its
233	        // duration.
234	        let (parser_theory, in_file) = {
235	            let inner = self.inner.lock();
236	            let entry = inner.by_idx.get(&idx)
237	                .ok_or_else(|| format!("theory index {} not found", idx))?;
238	            if let Some(ps) = &entry.proof_state {
239	                return Ok(ps.clone());
240	            }
241	            (entry.parser_theory.clone(), entry.origin.label())
242	        };
243	        let ps = Arc::new(ProofState::new(
244	            &parser_theory, &cfg.maude_path, cfg.stop_on_trace, &in_file)?);
245	        // Re-lock and double-check: another thread may have built (and
246	        // stored) the proof state while we held no lock.  If so, prefer
247	        // the already-stored one so all callers share a single instance.
248	        let mut inner = self.inner.lock();
249	        let entry = inner.by_idx.get_mut(&idx)
250	            .ok_or_else(|| format!("theory index {} not found", idx))?;
251	        if let Some(existing) = &entry.proof_state {
252	            return Ok(existing.clone());
253	        }
254	        entry.proof_state = Some(ps.clone());
255	        Ok(ps)
256	    }
257	}
258	
259	/// App-wide state, used by every handler.
260	pub struct AppState {
261	    pub cfg: crate::ServerConfig,
262	    pub store: TheoryStore,
263	}

1 // Currently GPL 3.0 until granted permission by the following authors: 2 // jdreier, meiersi, arcz, felixlinker, kevinmorio, rsasse, rkunnema, 3 // Kanakanajm, cascremers, YannColomb, symphorien, beschmi, 4 // racoucho1u, PhilipLukertWork, yavivanov, addap, Mathias-AURAND, 5 // BTom-GH, xaDxelA, Nynko, felixonmars, Azurios-git, 6 // Esslingen-Security-Privacy, gilcu3, katrielalex, and other minor 7 // contributors (see upstream git history) 8 // Ported from upstream tamarin-prover sources: 9 // lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/Solver/ProofMethod.hs, 10 // lib/theory/src/Theory/Proof.hs, 11 // lib/utils/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs, src/Main/TheoryLoader.hs, 12 // src/Web/Handler.hs, src/Web/Theory.hs, src/Web/Types.hs 13 14 //! Per-theory HTTP handlers. Each one looks up the theory by idx, 15 //! parses the trailing wildcard path, and emits HTML or the JSON 16 //! envelope the frontend expects. 17 18 // the `HashMap` here are 19 // query-parameter maps (axum `Query` extractors + a keyed graph-options 20 // lookup), consumed by key only — never iterated into output — and off the 21 // batch `--prove` byte-parity surface (server UI only). std kept: axum's 22 // `Query>` requires the std type. 23 #![allow(clippy::disallowed_types)] 24 25 use std::sync::Arc; 26 27 use axum::{ 28 extract::{Path, Query, State}, 29 http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode, header}, 30 response::{IntoResponse, Response}, 31 }; 32 use std::collections::HashMap; 33 use serde_json::Value; 34 35 use crate::handlers::{html_response, json_resp, path_parse, text_response, theory_html}; 36 use crate::state::AppState; 37 38 use tamarin_theory::constraint::solver::search::NodeStatus; 39 40 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 41 // Helpers 42 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 43 44 /// Haskell's `notFound` returns a 404 HTML page. We mirror that so the 45 /// frontend's `server.handleResponseError` triggers the right branch. 46 /// Used for non-JSON live routes (`overview`, `download`, `source`, 47 /// `message`, `unload`). 48 pub fn missing_idx_html(idx: usize) -> Response { 49 let body = format!( 50 "\nNot Found\ 51

Not Found

Theory index {} not found.

", 52 idx); 53 let mut headers = HeaderMap::new(); 54 headers.insert(header::CONTENT_TYPE, "text/html; charset=utf-8".parse().unwrap()); 55 (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, headers, body).into_response() 56 } 57 58 /// Parse the trailing wildcard path. Returns `None` on UNPARSEABLE 59 /// input, mirroring Haskell's Yesod `PathMultiPiece TheoryPath` 60 /// instance (`fromPathMultiPiece = parseTheoryPath`, 61 /// `src/Web/Types.hs:650-652`): when `parseTheoryPath` returns 62 /// `Nothing`, Yesod routing yields `notFound` (404) BEFORE the handler 63 /// runs, so a malformed path 404s on every theory route. Callers must 64 /// map `None` to [`not_found_response`]. Note the legitimate help view 65 /// (`/help`) parses to `TheoryPath::Help`, so it is NOT affected. 66 fn parse_path(raw: &str) -> Option { 67 path_parse::parse(raw) 68 } 69 70 /// Generic 404 used when the trailing path doesn't parse. Mirrors 71 /// Yesod's routing-level `notFound` (a plain 404) — matches the 72 /// already-fixed `graph` handler's `(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Not 73 /// Found")` response. 74 fn not_found_response() -> Response { 75 (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Not Found").into_response() 76 } 77 78 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 79 // Overview / main view 80 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 81 82 /// `GET /thy/trace//overview/*path` — full framed page. 83 pub async fn interactive_overview( 84 State(state): State>, 85 Path((idx, raw_path)): Path<(usize, String)>, 86 ) -> Response { 87 if state.store.get(idx).is_none() { 88 // Haskell's `notFound` returns 404 HTML; our overview is HTML 89 // too so we match exactly. 90 return missing_idx_html(idx); 91 } 92 let Some(path) = parse_path(&raw_path) else { 93 return not_found_response(); 94 }; 95 // The full framed page ALWAYS renders the left-pane proof-state tree 96 // (`proof_state`), whose rule count (incl. the ISend/IRecv intruder 97 // members of `crProtocol`) and raw/refined source-case annotations come 98 // from the closed-theory `ProofContext`. HS has these at theory-close 99 // time for every page; RS builds the context lazily, so we must ensure it 100 // here regardless of the center path — otherwise a frame whose center 101 // needs no proof state (help/edit/add/delete) would show `(0 cases)` and a 102 // proto-only rule count. Best-effort: a Maude failure leaves the counts 103 // as-is. 104 let _ = state.store.ensure_proof_state(idx, &state.cfg); 105 let Some(entry) = state.store.get(idx) else { 106 return missing_idx_html(idx); 107 }; 108 html_response(theory_html::overview_page(&entry, &path)) 109 } 110 111 /// `GET /thy/trace//main/*path` — AJAX-only JsonHtml content 112 /// (no framing). Missing idx returns 404 HTML to match Haskell's 113 /// `withTheory` / `notFound` (see `src/Web/Handler.hs:660-666`). 114 /// 115 /// Special-cases the `TheoryMethod` path (Haskell `getTheoryPathMR` → 116 /// `applyMethodAtPath`): we look up the ranked applicable methods at 117 /// the indicated proof node, apply the requested one, allocate a fresh 118 /// theory idx for the post-step state, and return a `{redirect}` JSON 119 /// envelope pointing at `/thy/trace//overview/proof/...`. 120 pub async fn theory_path_main( 121 State(state): State>, 122 Path((idx, raw_path)): Path<(usize, String)>, 123 ) -> Response { 124 if state.store.get(idx).is_none() { 125 return missing_idx_html(idx); 126 } 127 let Some(path) = parse_path(&raw_path) else { 128 return not_found_response(); 129 }; 130 // Method paths mutate the proof tree; dispatch separately. 131 if let path_parse::TheoryPath::Method { lemma, idx: method_nr, sub } = &path { 132 return apply_method_and_redirect( 133 &state, idx, lemma, *method_nr, sub).into_response(); 134 } 135 materialise_proof_state_if_needed(&state, idx, &path); 136 let Some(entry) = state.store.get(idx) else { 137 return missing_idx_html(idx); 138 }; 139 let title = title_for(&entry, &path); 140 let body = theory_html::path_html(&entry, &path); 141 json_resp::html(title, body).into_response() 142 } 143 144 /// Build the `overview/proof` redirect URL for lemma `lemma` at proof 145 /// path `sub` under theory `idx`. Percent-encodes the lemma segment 146 /// and each sub segment via the shared `path_parse` helpers, matching 147 /// Yesod `getUrlRender`. `sub == &[]` yields the bare 148 /// `.../overview/proof/` root URL (`encode_sub_path(&[]) == ""`). 149 fn overview_proof_url(idx: usize, lemma: &str, sub: &[String]) -> String { 150 let mut u = format!( 151 "/thy/trace/{}/overview/proof/{}", 152 idx, path_parse::url_path_escape(lemma)); 153 u.push_str(&path_parse::encode_sub_path(sub)); 154 u 155 } 156 157 /// Apply ranked method `method_nr` (1-based) at proof path `sub` in 158 /// lemma `lemma`'s tree. Allocates a fresh idx for the post-step 159 /// state and returns a JsonRedirect pointing at the resulting 160 /// `overview/proof//` URL. Mirrors Haskell's 161 /// `applyMethodAtPath` + `modifyTheory` flow in 162 /// `src/Web/Handler.hs:1013-1015` and `src/Web/Theory.hs:80-94`. 163 fn apply_method_and_redirect( 164 state: &AppState, 165 idx: usize, 166 lemma: &str, 167 method_nr: usize, 168 sub: &[String], 169 ) -> axum::Json { 170 // Ensure the proof state at the *source* idx is built (so we can 171 // navigate to the sub-path and rank candidate methods there). 172 let src_ps = match state.store.ensure_proof_state(idx, &state.cfg) { 173 Ok(p) => p, 174 Err(e) => return json_resp::alert(format!("proof state init failed: {}", e)), 175 }; 176 // Look up the system at the requested path. 177 let sys_at_path = match src_ps.get_system_at(lemma, sub) { 178 Some(s) => s, 179 None => return json_resp::alert(format!( 180 "no system at path {:?} in lemma {}", sub, lemma)), 181 }; 182 // Pick the N-th ranked method (1-based). Filter to only those 183 // methods whose `exec_proof_method` succeeds — matches Haskell's 184 // `rankProofMethods` → `execMethods` (`ProofMethod.hs:653-668`) 185 // semantics, and matches the user-visible numbering produced by 186 // `write_applicable_methods` (which applies the same filter). 187 // Without filtering here the numbering would drift on Sorry/no-op 188 // candidates that the UI omits. 189 let method = { 190 // `exec_proof_method` below resolves user fun symbols via 191 // thread-locals — install them (tokio workers start empty; see 192 // `ProofState::user_funs`). 193 let _user_funs_guard = src_ps.install_user_funs(); 194 let mut ctx_guard = src_ps.ctx.lock(); 195 // Install this lemma's per-lemma `use_induction`/`heuristic` into the 196 // shared ctx BEFORE ranking, so the method-index → method mapping 197 // matches HS (and the numbering `write_applicable_methods` displays). 198 // Without this the mapping ranks under `AvoidInduction`/`Smart`, so a 199 // `[use_induction]` lemma's method `1` resolves to the wrong method. 200 src_ps.install_lemma_settings(&mut ctx_guard, lemma); 201 // Haskell `applyMethodAtPath` ranks with `useHeuristic heuristic 202 // (length proofPath)` (Web/Theory.hs:84-89); the depth selects 203 // which ranking of a multi-ranking heuristic is active 204 // (`rankings !! (depth mod n)`, ProofMethod.hs:583-590). Same body as the `source_` handler, 968 // different content-type/disposition. 969 let _ = state.store.ensure_proof_state(idx, &state.cfg); 970 let Some(entry) = state.store.get(idx) else { 971 return missing_idx_html(idx); 972 }; 973 let mut headers = HeaderMap::new(); 974 headers.insert(header::CONTENT_TYPE, "application/octet-stream".parse().unwrap()); 975 headers.insert( 976 header::CONTENT_DISPOSITION, 977 format!("attachment; filename=\"{}\"", name).parse().unwrap(), 978 ); 979 (StatusCode::OK, headers, render_theory_source(&entry)).into_response() 980 } 981 982 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 983 // Stubs (501) for features not yet ported. 984 // --------------------------------------------------------------------- 985 986 fn stub_alert(what: &str) -> axum::Json { 987 json_resp::alert(format!( 988 "{} is not yet implemented in the Rust port (frontend stub)", what)) 989 } 990 991 /// `GET /thy/trace//next/
/*path` — 992 /// Compute the next theory-path under `section ∈ { normal, smart }` 993 /// and return its `/main/...` URL as `text/plain`. 994 /// 995 /// Mirrors Haskell `getNextTheoryPathR` (`src/Web/Handler.hs:1444-1455`): 996 /// 1. parse `path` into a TheoryPath 997 /// 2. call `nextThyPath` or `nextSmartThyPath` 998 /// 3. render `TheoryPathMR idx ` as a URL string 999 /// 1000 /// Our solver doesn't yet maintain the proof tree, so for `TheoryProof` 1001 /// the "next sibling" is the same path (matches Haskell's behaviour 1002 /// when no sibling exists; see `getNextElement`). Other path 1003 /// transitions are pure (no proof state needed) and match Haskell. 1004 pub async fn next_path( 1005 State(state): State>, 1006 Path((idx, section, raw_path)): Path<(usize, String, String)>, 1007 ) -> Response { 1008 let Some(entry) = state.store.get(idx) else { 1009 return missing_idx_html(idx); 1010 }; 1011 let Some(path) = parse_path(&raw_path) else { 1012 return not_found_response(); 1013 }; 1014 let new_path = next_theory_path(&path, §ion, &entry); 1015 let url = render_main_url(idx, &new_path); 1016 text_response(url) 1017 } 1018 1019 /// `GET /thy/trace//prev/
/*path` — symmetric to `next`. 1020 pub async fn prev_path( 1021 State(state): State>, 1022 Path((idx, section, raw_path)): Path<(usize, String, String)>, 1023 ) -> Response { 1024 let Some(entry) = state.store.get(idx) else { 1025 return missing_idx_html(idx); 1026 }; 1027 let Some(path) = parse_path(&raw_path) else { 1028 return not_found_response(); 1029 }; 1030 let new_path = prev_theory_path(&path, §ion, &entry); 1031 let url = render_main_url(idx, &new_path); 1032 text_response(url) 1033 } 1034 1035 /// Haskell `nextThyPath`/`nextSmartThyPath`. 1036 /// 1037 /// The `section` argument is matched verbatim against the strings 1038 /// `"normal"` / `"smart"`; any other value falls through to `const id` 1039 /// (no-op) per Haskell's `next _ = const id` in 1040 /// `src/Web/Handler.hs:1452-1455`. That means e.g. `next/main/help` 1041 /// returns the SAME path back — used by the frontend when the user 1042 /// presses arrow keys outside the proof tree. 1043 fn next_theory_path( 1044 p: &path_parse::TheoryPath, 1045 section: &str, 1046 entry: &crate::state::TheoryEntry, 1047 ) -> path_parse::TheoryPath { 1048 // HS `getNextTheoryPathR` (`Handler.hs:1452-1455`): `next "normal" = 1049 // nextThyPath`, `next "smart" = nextSmartThyPath`, everything else 1050 // `const id` (no-op). 1	//! Route model for the interactive web UI.
2	//!
3	//! Every observed request path has the shape
4	//! `/thy/<theory-kind>/<index>/<handler>/<args…>` where:
5	//! * `<theory-kind>` is the analysis kind — only `trace` appears in the corpus
6	//!   (a `diff` kind is plausible but unobserved).
7	//! * `<index>` is either `#` (the "current" theory version) or a decimal number.
8	//! * `<handler>` selects the response family. Observed handlers and their
9	//!   response `kind`:
10	//!     - `main/…`                    -> JSON envelope (`{html,title}` / `{redirect}`)
11	//!     - `overview/…`                -> full-page HTML
12	//!     - `intdot/…`                  -> HTML mini-page
13	//!     - `interactive-graph-def/…`   -> DOT
14	//!     - `next/…`, `prev/…`          -> text (a navigation URL)
15	//!     - `autoprove/…`               -> JSON (`{redirect}`), or text on timeout
16	//!     - `source`, `message`         -> text (theory source)
17	//!
18	//! Under `main`, the sub-handlers are: `help`, `message`, `rules`, `tactic`,
19	//! `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}`, `lemma/{name}`, `add/{pos}`,
20	//! `edit/{name}`, `delete/{name}`, `method/{lemma}/{n}`, and
21	//! `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (the proof path is a sequence of case-name segments,
22	//! the root being `_`).
23	//!
24	//! This module parses a path into a structured value; it is descriptive (the
25	//! route grammar as observed), not a dispatcher.
26	
27	/// Theory version selector.
28	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
29	pub enum Index {
30	    /// `#` — the server's "current" theory version.
31	    Current,
32	    /// An explicit decimal version index.
33	    Num(u64),
34	}
35	
36	impl Index {
37	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Index> {
38	        if s == "#" {
39	            Some(Index::Current)
40	        } else {
41	            s.parse::<u64>().ok().map(Index::Num)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// A `main/*` sub-handler.
47	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
48	pub enum Main {
49	    Help,
50	    Message,
51	    Rules,
52	    Tactic,
53	    Cases { refined: bool, level: usize, n: usize },
54	    Lemma(String),
55	    Add(String),
56	    Edit(String),
57	    Delete(String),
58	    /// Apply proof method `n` at the node identified by `path` (a `/`-joined
59	    /// sequence of case-name segments, root `_`). The method number precedes the
60	    /// path in the URL: `method/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]` (observed live [L8] and in
61	    /// 67988 body links [Q032]).
62	    Method { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
63	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
64	    /// Observational-equivalence proof view (`main/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]`),
65	    /// the diff-mode analogue of `Proof`.
66	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
67	    /// Apply a diff proof method (`main/diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]`), the
68	    /// diff-mode analogue of `Method`.
69	    DiffMethod { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
70	    /// The diff construction/deconstruction rules view (`main/diffrules`).
71	    DiffRules,
72	    /// Any unrecognized `main/*` tail.
73	    Other(Vec<String>),
74	}
75	
76	/// The selected handler and its arguments.
77	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
78	pub enum Handler {
79	    Source,
80	    Message,
81	    Main(Main),
82	    Overview(Vec<String>),
83	    Intdot(Vec<String>),
84	    InteractiveGraphDef(Vec<String>),
85	    Next(Vec<String>),
86	    Prev(Vec<String>),
87	    Autoprove(Vec<String>),
88	    /// Autoprove a single diff lemma (`autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/…`).
89	    AutoproveDiff(Vec<String>),
90	    /// Autoprove all lemmas (`autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}`).
91	    AutoproveAll(Vec<String>),
92	    /// Re-read the theory from disk in place (`reload`; POST).
93	    Reload,
94	    /// Download the theory source (`download/{file}`; GET). The filename is a
95	    /// decorative URL segment — the body is always the current theory's source.
96	    Download(String),
97	    /// Append modified lemmas to the on-disk file (`get_and_append/{file}`; POST).
98	    GetAndAppend(String),
99	    /// A structural-edit form target (`edit/{verb}/{name}`; POST). The tail is the
100	    /// `{verb}/{name}` remainder.
101	    Edit(Vec<String>),
102	    /// Delete a theory path (`del/path/{theory-path…}`; GET). The tail is the
103	    /// theory-path segments after the fixed `path` literal. It is parsed into a
104	    /// [`ThyPath`] by the dispatcher (mode-aware): an unparseable tail is a `404`,
105	    /// a parseable one with a non-GET method is a `405`.
106	    DelPath(Vec<String>),
107	    /// Verify a theory path (`verify/{theory-path…}`; GET; trace theories only).
108	    /// The tail is the theory-path segments, parsed into a [`ThyPath`] by the
109	    /// dispatcher.
110	    Verify(Vec<String>),
111	    /// Unrecognized handler with its raw tail.
112	    Other { name: String, tail: Vec<String> },
113	}
114	
115	/// A parsed route.
116	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
117	pub struct Route {
118	    pub theory_kind: String,
119	    pub index: Index,
120	    pub handler: Handler,
121	}
122	
123	fn owned(v: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> {
124	    v.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()
125	}
126	
127	fn parse_main(tail: &[&str]) -> Main {
128	    match tail {
129	        ["help"] => Main::Help,
130	        ["message"] => Main::Message,
131	        ["rules"] => Main::Rules,
132	        ["diffrules"] => Main::DiffRules,
133	        ["tactic"] => Main::Tactic,
134	        ["cases", kind, level, n]
135	            if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
136	                && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
137	                && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
138	        {
139	            Main::Cases {
140	                refined: *kind == "refined",
141	                level: level.parse().unwrap(),
142	                n: n.parse().unwrap(),
143	            }
144	        }
145	        ["lemma", name] => Main::Lemma((*name).to_string()),
146	        ["add", pos] => Main::Add((*pos).to_string()),
147	        ["edit", name] => Main::Edit((*name).to_string()),
148	        ["delete", name] => Main::Delete((*name).to_string()),
149	        ["method", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::Method {
150	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
151	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
152	            path: owned(rest),
153	        },
154	        ["diffMethod", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::DiffMethod {
155	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
156	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
157	            path: owned(rest),
158	        },
159	        ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Main::DiffProof {
160	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
161	            path: owned(rest),
162	        },
163	        [proof, lemma, rest @ ..] if *proof == "proof" => Main::Proof {
164	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
165	            path: owned(rest),
166	        },
167	        _ => Main::Other(owned(tail)),
168	    }
169	}
170	
171	impl Route {
172	    /// Parse a request path such as `/thy/trace/#/main/proof/exec/_/B_2`.
173	    /// Returns `None` if the path is not under `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…`.
174	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Option<Route> {
175	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
176	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
177	        // Need at least: thy / kind / index / handler
178	        if segs.len() < 4 || segs[0] != "thy" {
179	            return None;
180	        }
181	        let theory_kind = segs[1].to_string();
182	        let index = Index::parse(segs[2])?;
183	        let handler_name = segs[3];
184	        let tail = &segs[4..];
185	        let handler = match handler_name {
186	            "source" => Handler::Source,
187	            "message" => Handler::Message,
188	            "main" => Handler::Main(parse_main(tail)),
189	            "overview" => Handler::Overview(owned(tail)),
190	            "intdot" => Handler::Intdot(owned(tail)),
191	            "interactive-graph-def" => Handler::InteractiveGraphDef(owned(tail)),
192	            "next" => Handler::Next(owned(tail)),
193	            "prev" => Handler::Prev(owned(tail)),
194	            "autoprove" => Handler::Autoprove(owned(tail)),
195	            "autoproveDiff" => Handler::AutoproveDiff(owned(tail)),
196	            "autoproveAll" => Handler::AutoproveAll(owned(tail)),
197	            "reload" => Handler::Reload,
198	            "download" => match tail {
199	                [file] => Handler::Download((*file).to_string()),
200	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "download".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
201	            },
202	            "get_and_append" => match tail {
203	                [file] => Handler::GetAndAppend((*file).to_string()),
204	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "get_and_append".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
205	            },
206	            "edit" => Handler::Edit(owned(tail)),
207	            // `del/path/<theory-path…>` — the fixed `path` literal must follow.
208	            "del" => match tail {
209	                ["path", rest @ ..] => Handler::DelPath(owned(rest)),
210	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "del".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
211	            },
212	            "verify" => Handler::Verify(owned(tail)),
213	            other => Handler::Other {
214	                name: other.to_string(),
215	                tail: owned(tail),
216	            },
217	        };
218	        Some(Route {
219	            theory_kind,
220	            index,
221	            handler,
222	        })
223	    }
224	}
225	
226	/// The full request surface: the top-level (non-`/thy`) routes plus the
227	/// theory-scoped `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…` routes.
228	///
229	/// `Toplevel::parse` takes the URL **path** only (any `?query` must be split off
230	/// by the caller and passed separately to the dispatcher).
231	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
232	pub enum Toplevel {
233	    /// `/` — the index page (GET) / theory upload (POST).
234	    Root,
235	    /// `/robots.txt`.
236	    Robots,
237	    /// `/favicon.ico` — a `303` redirect to the static icon.
238	    Favicon,
239	    /// `/kill` — cancel a running proof search (requires a `path` query arg).
240	    Kill,
241	    /// `/static/<path…>` — a filesystem-backed asset.
242	    Static(Vec<String>),
243	    /// A theory-scoped route.
244	    Thy(Route),
245	    /// Anything else (→ 404).
246	    Other(String),
247	}
248	
249	impl Toplevel {
250	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Toplevel {
251	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
252	        if trimmed.is_empty() {
253	            return Toplevel::Root;
254	        }
255	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
256	        match segs[0] {
257	            "robots.txt" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Robots,
258	            "favicon.ico" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Favicon,
259	            "kill" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Kill,
260	            "static" => Toplevel::Static(owned(&segs[1..])),
261	            "thy" => match Route::parse(path) {
262	                Some(r) => Toplevel::Thy(r),
263	                None => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
264	            },
265	            _ => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
266	        }
267	    }
268	}
269	
270	/// A parsed `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path…]` request.
271	///
272	/// Observed variant matrix (corpus [Q030], keyboard help [Q031]): `strategy` is
273	/// `idfs` (solve/prove) or `characterize` (characterization, e.g. exists-trace);
274	/// `bound` is a depth bound (`0` = unbounded — the `a`/`A`/`s`/`S` shortcuts; `5`
275	/// = bounded — the `b`/`B` shortcuts); `all_solutions` is the `True`/`False` flag
276	/// (`False` = stop after the first solution, the lowercase shortcuts; `True` =
277	/// search for all solutions, the uppercase shortcuts).
278	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
279	pub struct Autoprove {
280	    pub strategy: String,
281	    pub bound: u64,
282	    pub all_solutions: bool,
283	    pub lemma: String,
284	    pub path: Vec<String>,
285	}
286	
287	impl Autoprove {
288	    /// Parse the tail of an `autoprove/…` route (everything after `autoprove/`).
289	    pub fn parse(tail: &[String]) -> Option<Autoprove> {
290	        match tail {
291	            [strategy, bound, flag, proof, lemma, rest @ ..] if proof == "proof" => {
292	                Some(Autoprove {
293	                    strategy: strategy.clone(),
294	                    bound: bound.parse().ok()?,
295	                    all_solutions: parse_bool(flag)?,
296	                    lemma: lemma.clone(),
297	                    path: rest.to_vec(),
298	                })
299	            }
300	            _ => None,
301	        }
302	    }
303	}
304	
305	/// A parsed `autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` request
306	/// (observational-equivalence autoprove). Unlike [`Autoprove`], the diff form
307	/// carries **no** all-solutions flag (observed in the diff-proof body links).
308	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
309	pub struct AutoproveDiff {
310	    pub strategy: String,
311	    pub bound: u64,
312	    pub lemma: String,
313	    pub path: Vec<String>,
314	}
315	
316	impl AutoproveDiff {
317	    /// Parse the tail of an `autoproveDiff/…` route.
318	    pub fn parse(tail: &[String]) -> Option<AutoproveDiff> {
319	        match tail {
320	            [strategy, bound, marker, lemma, rest @ ..] if marker == "diffProof" => {
321	                Some(AutoproveDiff {
322	                    strategy: strategy.clone(),
323	                    bound: bound.parse().ok()?,
324	                    lemma: lemma.clone(),
325	                    path: rest.to_vec(),
326	                })
327	            }
328	            _ => None,
329	        }
330	    }
331	}
332	
333	/// A parsed `autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}` request (autoprove every lemma).
334	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
335	pub struct AutoproveAll {
336	    pub strategy: String,
337	    pub bound: u64,
338	}
339	
340	impl AutoproveAll {
341	    /// Parse the tail of an `autoproveAll/…` route.
342	    pub fn parse(tail: &[String]) -> Option<AutoproveAll> {
343	        match tail {
344	            [strategy, bound] => Some(AutoproveAll {
345	                strategy: strategy.clone(),
346	                bound: bound.parse().ok()?,
347	            }),
348	            _ => None,
349	        }
350	    }
351	}
352	
353	/// Navigation direction for `next`/`prev`.
354	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone, Copy)]
355	pub enum NavDir {
356	    Next,
357	    Prev,
358	}
359	
360	/// A parsed `next|prev/{mode}/proof/{lemma}` request. `mode` (`normal` observed;
361	/// the server also accepts other tokens and passes them to the prover) selects the
362	/// prover's traversal function.
363	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
364	pub struct Nav {
365	    pub dir: NavDir,
366	    pub mode: String,
367	    pub lemma: String,
368	}
369	
370	impl Nav {
371	    pub fn parse(dir: NavDir, tail: &[String]) -> Option<Nav> {
372	        match tail {
373	            [mode, proof, lemma] if proof == "proof" => Some(Nav {
374	                dir,
375	                mode: mode.clone(),
376	                lemma: lemma.clone(),
377	            }),
378	            _ => None,
379	        }
380	    }
381	}
382	
383	/// The full-page `overview/*` views. `help` and `proof/…` appear in the crawl;
384	/// `edit/{name}` and `add/{pos}` appear only as POST-redirect (303) targets
385	/// (live [L12]).
386	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
387	pub enum OverviewView {
388	    Help,
389	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
390	    /// The diff-mode proof view (`overview/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]`), the target
391	    /// of a `diffMethod`/`autoproveDiff` redirect.
392	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
393	    Edit(String),
394	    Add(String),
395	    /// The delete-confirmation view (`overview/delete/{name}`), the target of a
396	    /// failed (lemma-not-found) delete.
397	    Delete(String),
398	    Other(Vec<String>),
399	}
400	
401	impl OverviewView {
402	    pub fn parse(tail: &[String]) -> OverviewView {
403	        match tail.iter().map(String::as_str).collect::<Vec<_>>().as_slice() {
404	            ["help"] => OverviewView::Help,
405	            ["proof", lemma, rest @ ..] => OverviewView::Proof {
406	                lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
407	                path: rest.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
408	            },
409	            ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => OverviewView::DiffProof {
410	                lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
411	                path: rest.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
412	            },
413	            ["edit", name] => OverviewView::Edit((*name).to_string()),
414	            ["add", pos] => OverviewView::Add((*pos).to_string()),
415	            ["delete", name] => OverviewView::Delete((*name).to_string()),
416	            _ => OverviewView::Other(tail.to_vec()),
417	        }
418	    }
419	}
420	
421	/// The structural-edit verb of a POST `edit/{verb}/{name}` form target
422	/// (live [L12]/[L13]).
423	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
424	pub enum EditVerb {
425	    Edit,
426	    Add,
427	    Delete,
428	}
429	
430	impl EditVerb {
431	    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<EditVerb> {
432	        match s {
433	            "edit" => Some(EditVerb::Edit),
434	            "add" => Some(EditVerb::Add),
435	            "delete" => Some(EditVerb::Delete),
436	            _ => None,
437	        }
438	    }
439	}
440	
441	/// A theory navigation path, as accepted by the `del/path/…` and `verify/…`
442	/// routes. The accepted heads are **mode-dependent**: a `trace` theory accepts
443	/// `help` · `message` · `rules` · `tactic` · `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}` ·
444	/// `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` · `method/{lemma}/{n}[/seg…]` ·
445	/// `add/{pos}` · `edit/{name}` · `delete/{name}`; an `equiv` (diff) theory accepts
446	/// `help` · `diffrules` · `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` · `diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/…]`.
447	/// A tail outside the grammar does not parse — the route then answers `404`.
448	///
449	/// Only the lemma and proof nodes carry data the dispatcher needs; every other
450	/// accepted head collapses to [`ThyPath::Other`].
451	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
452	pub enum ThyPath {
453	    /// `lemma/{name}` (trace).
454	    Lemma(String),
455	    /// `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` (trace).
456	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
457	    /// `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` (equiv).
458	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
459	    /// Any other accepted theory path (a navigable view that is neither a lemma
460	    /// nor a proof node).
461	    Other,
462	}
463	
464	impl ThyPath {
465	    /// Parse a theory-path tail. `diff` selects the equiv (diff-theory) grammar.
466	    /// Returns `None` for a tail outside the grammar (the caller then answers a
467	    /// `404`).
468	    pub fn parse(segs: &[String], diff: bool) -> Option<ThyPath> {
469	        let s: Vec<&str> = segs.iter().map(String::as_str).collect();
470	        if diff {
471	            match s.as_slice() {
472	                ["help"] | ["diffrules"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
473	                ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::DiffProof {
474	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
475	                    path: owned(rest),
476	                }),
477	                ["diffMethod", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
478	                _ => None,
479	            }
480	        } else {
481	            match s.as_slice() {
482	                ["help"] | ["message"] | ["rules"] | ["tactic"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
483	                ["cases", kind, level, n]
484	                    if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
485	                        && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
486	                        && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
487	                {
488	                    Some(ThyPath::Other)
489	                }
490	                ["lemma", name] => Some(ThyPath::Lemma((*name).to_string())),
491	                ["proof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::Proof {
492	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
493	                    path: owned(rest),
494	                }),
495	                ["method", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
496	                ["add", _] | ["edit", _] | ["delete", _] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
497	                _ => None,
498	            }
499	        }
500	    }
501	}
502	
503	fn parse_bool(s: &str) -> Option<bool> {
504	    match s {
505	        "True" => Some(true),
506	        "False" => Some(false),
507	        _ => None,
508	    }
509	}
510	
511	#[cfg(test)]
512	mod tests {
513	    use super::*;
514	
515	    #[test]
516	    fn parses_main_rules() {
517	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/#/main/rules").unwrap();
518	        assert_eq!(r.theory_kind, "trace");
519	        assert_eq!(r.index, Index::Current);
520	        assert_eq!(r.handler, Handler::Main(Main::Rules));
521	    }
522	
523	    #[test]
524	    fn parses_cases_with_numbers() {
525	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/main/cases/refined/0/2").unwrap();
526	        assert_eq!(r.index, Index::Num(1));
527	        assert_eq!(
528	            r.handler,
529	            Handler::Main(Main::Cases { refined: true, level: 0, n: 2 })
530	        );
531	    }
532	
533	    #[test]
534	    fn parses_proof_path() {
535	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/3/main/proof/exec/_/B_2").unwrap();
536	        assert_eq!(
537	            r.handler,
538	            Handler::Main(Main::Proof {
539	                lemma: "exec".to_string(),
540	                path: vec!["_".to_string(), "B_2".to_string()],
541	            })
542	        );
543	    }
544	
545	    #[test]
546	    fn parses_method_and_dot_and_text() {
547	        assert_eq!(
548	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/#/main/method/exec/1").unwrap().handler,
549	            Handler::Main(Main::Method { lemma: "exec".to_string(), n: 1, path: vec![] })
550	        );
551	        assert_eq!(
552	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/3/main/method/exec/2/_/B_2").unwrap().handler,
553	            Handler::Main(Main::Method {
554	                lemma: "exec".to_string(),
555	                n: 2,
556	                path: vec!["_".to_string(), "B_2".to_string()],
557	            })
558	        );
559	        assert!(matches!(
560	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/#/interactive-graph-def/proof/exec").unwrap().handler,
561	            Handler::InteractiveGraphDef(_)
562	        ));
563	        assert_eq!(
564	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/#/source").unwrap().handler,
565	            Handler::Source
566	        );
567	    }
568	
569	    #[test]
570	    fn rejects_non_thy() {
571	        assert!(Route::parse("/static/css/x.css").is_none());
572	        assert!(Route::parse("/").is_none());
573	    }
574	
575	    #[test]
576	    fn parses_autoprove_variants() {
577	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/types").unwrap();
578	        let tail = match r.handler {
579	            Handler::Autoprove(t) => t,
580	            _ => panic!("not autoprove"),
581	        };
582	        assert_eq!(
583	            Autoprove::parse(&tail).unwrap(),
584	            Autoprove {
585	                strategy: "idfs".into(),
586	                bound: 0,
587	                all_solutions: false,
588	                lemma: "types".into(),
589	                path: vec![],
590	            }
591	        );
592	        // characterize, bounded, all-solutions, with a path.
593	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/autoprove/characterize/5/True/proof/L/_/B_2").unwrap();
594	        let tail = match r.handler {
595	            Handler::Autoprove(t) => t,
596	            _ => panic!(),
597	        };
598	        let ap = Autoprove::parse(&tail).unwrap();
599	        assert_eq!(ap.strategy, "characterize");
600	        assert_eq!(ap.bound, 5);
601	        assert!(ap.all_solutions);
602	        assert_eq!(ap.path, vec!["_".to_string(), "B_2".to_string()]);
603	    }
604	
605	    #[test]
606	    fn parses_del_path_and_verify_handlers() {
607	        // del/path strips the fixed `path` literal into the theory-path tail.
608	        assert_eq!(
609	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/del/path/lemma/debug").unwrap().handler,
610	            Handler::DelPath(vec!["lemma".into(), "debug".into()])
611	        );
612	        // `del` without the `path` literal is not a del/path route.
613	        assert!(matches!(
614	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/del/lemma/debug").unwrap().handler,
615	            Handler::Other { .. }
616	        ));
617	        assert_eq!(
618	            Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/verify/proof/debug/_/B_2").unwrap().handler,
619	            Handler::Verify(vec!["proof".into(), "debug".into(), "_".into(), "B_2".into()])
620	        );
621	    }
622	
623	    #[test]
624	    fn thy_path_grammar_is_mode_dependent() {
625	        let sp = |s: &str| s.split('/').map(String::from).collect::<Vec<_>>();
626	        // Trace grammar.
627	        assert_eq!(ThyPath::parse(&sp("lemma/debug"), false), Some(ThyPath::Lemma("debug".into())));
628	        assert_eq!(
629	            ThyPath::parse(&sp("proof/debug/_/ONE"), false),
630	            Some(ThyPath::Proof { lemma: "debug".into(), path: vec!["_".into(), "ONE".into()] })
631	        );
632	        for ok in ["help", "message", "rules", "tactic", "cases/raw/0/0", "method/debug/1", "add/x", "edit/x", "delete/x"] {
633	            assert_eq!(ThyPath::parse(&sp(ok), false), Some(ThyPath::Other), "trace {ok}");
634	        }
635	        for bad in ["sources", "cases", "diffrules", "diffProof/debug", "x", "foo/bar"] {
636	            assert_eq!(ThyPath::parse(&sp(bad), false), None, "trace {bad} should 404");
637	        }
638	        // Equiv grammar: diff heads parse, trace heads do not.
639	        assert_eq!(
640	            ThyPath::parse(&sp("diffProof/L/RHS"), true),
641	            Some(ThyPath::DiffProof { lemma: "L".into(), path: vec!["RHS".into()] })
642	        );
643	        for ok in ["help", "diffrules", "diffMethod/L/1"] {
644	            assert_eq!(ThyPath::parse(&sp(ok), true), Some(ThyPath::Other), "equiv {ok}");
645	        }
646	        for bad in ["rules", "message", "tactic", "lemma/L", "proof/L", "cases/raw/0/0", "add/x"] {
647	            assert_eq!(ThyPath::parse(&sp(bad), true), None, "equiv {bad} should 404");
648	        }
649	    }
650	
651	    #[test]
652	    fn parses_nav_and_overview_views() {
653	        let r = Route::parse("/thy/trace/1/next/normal/proof/Client_auth").unwrap();
654	        if let Handler::Next(t) = r.handler {
655	            assert_eq!(
656	                Nav::parse(NavDir::Next, &t).unwrap(),
657	                Nav { dir: NavDir::Next, mode: "normal".into(), lemma: "Client_auth".into() }
658	            );
659	        } else {
660	            panic!();
661	        }
662	        assert_eq!(OverviewView::parse(&owned(&["help"])), OverviewView::Help);
663	        assert_eq!(
664	            OverviewView::parse(&owned(&["edit", "L"])),
665	            OverviewView::Edit("L".into())
666	        );
667	        assert_eq!(
668	            OverviewView::parse(&owned(&["proof", "L", "_", "B_2"])),
669	            OverviewView::Proof { lemma: "L".into(), path: vec!["_".into(), "B_2".into()] }
670	        );
671	    }
672	}
